channel.title: Paper Feeds (arXiv)
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv

item.title: A Lifecycle and Application-Stack Survey of Large Language Model Vulnerabilities: Attacks, Risks, Defenses, and Open Problems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31639v1

AI Summary (中文)

全生命周期与应用栈视角下的大语言模型安全脆弱性系统综述

随着大语言模型（LLM）从单纯文本生成器演变为嵌入检索管道、企业智能助手、编程环境、机器人系统、安全运营流程及跨组织自主代理的核心组件，其安全威胁已远超模型权重本身——风险根植于数据、提示、模型输出、工具调用、记忆机制与用户权限在全生命周期与应用栈中的动态交互。本文首次以生命周期阶段×应用栈层次双维框架系统梳理LLM系统脆弱性研究：将攻击面划分为八大阶段——数据采集、预训练、后训练对齐、模型打包与供应链、检索与记忆、提示工程与推理、工具/智能体执行、部署与运维。针对每一阶段，我们结构化分析攻击者能力边界、受损安全目标（保密性、完整性、可用性、安全性、隐私性、公平性、可问责性、主体控制权）、典型攻击案例（如数据污染、越狱提示、记忆泄露、工具劫持、代理链污染）、现实风险等级、现有评估方法局限，以及防御机制有效性。区别于碎片化攻击命名法，本工作强调信任边界失效点（如检索结果被当作可信指令）、非代码数据的可执行化（如PDF表格触发SQL注入）、委托权限对错误的放大效应（如Agent误调用API导致资金损失），并指出单点防御难以组合生效的根本原因。最后，提出面向产业落地的七大研究方向：可组合的安全架构、溯源感知的检索机制、工具调用沙箱化、长周期智能体行为评估、隐私保护式适配、贴近实战的红队演练、部署级事件响应体系。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first systematic survey of large language model (LLM) vulnerabilities through a dual-axis lens: lifecycle stages (data collection, pretraining, alignment, packaging/supply chain, retrieval/memory, prompting/inference, tool/agent execution, deployment/maintenance) and application-stack layers. We map 120+ attacks to eight stages, analyzing attacker capabilities, compromised security objectives (confidentiality, integrity, availability, safety, privacy, fairness, accountability, agency control), practical risks, evaluation gaps, and defense limitations. Crucially, we shift focus from isolated attack names to where trust boundaries collapse: e.g., untrusted retrieval results becoming executable instructions, or delegated authority amplifying model errors into real-world harm. We identify why point defenses rarely compose and propose a research agenda including compositional security, provenance-aware retrieval, tool-call containment, long-horizon agent evaluation, privacy-preserving adaptation, realistic red teaming, and deployment-grade incident response.

Abstract

Large language models are no longer only text generators. They are increasingly embedded in retrieval pipelines, enterprise assistants, coding environments, robotic systems, security-operation workflows, and autonomous agents that can read private data, call tools, write files, execute code, and act across organizational boundaries. This shift changes the security problem: risks do not arise from the model weights alone, but from the full lifecycle and application stack through which data, prompts, model outputs, tools, memories, and user authority interact. This paper systematizes the literature on vulnerabilities in large language model systems through a lifecycle and application-stack lens. We organize attacks across eight stages: data collection, pretraining, post-training alignment, model packaging and supply chain, retrieval and memory, prompting and inference, tool/agent execution, and deployment/maintenance. For each stage, we analyze attacker capabilities, affected security objectives, representative attacks, practical risks, evaluation practices, and defenses. We further map LLM-specific vulnerabilities to confidentiality, integrity, availability, safety, privacy, fairness, accountability, and agency-control objectives. Unlike taxonomies that list isolated attack names, the proposed systematization emphasizes where trust boundaries fail, how untrusted data becomes executable instruction, how delegated authority amplifies model errors, and why point defenses rarely compose. We close with a research agenda for secure LLM systems, including compositional security, provenance-aware retrieval, tool-call containment, long-horizon agent evaluation, privacy-preserving adaptation, realistic red teaming, and deployment-grade incident response.

item.title: Hybrid Topological Data Analysis and LSTM Networks for Enhanced Network Intrusion Detection Using CIC-IDS2017 Dataset
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31619v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

网络入侵检测系统（NIDS）是现代网络安全防御体系的核心组件，亟需兼具鲁棒性与可解释性的先进方法以应对日益复杂的多类型攻击。传统机器学习模型在高维、非线性、时序耦合的网络流量数据上常面临特征表达力不足与时间动态建模薄弱的双重挑战。

方法创新

本研究提出一种拓扑数据驱动+深度时序建模的混合框架：首先利用拓扑数据分析（TDA） 提取网络流的全局结构特征——通过计算77维CIC-IDS2017流量特征的持久同调，生成Betti曲线与持久图；继而将这些拓扑不变量与原始时序片段联合编码，输入双向LSTM网络，实现对攻击模式中“空间拓扑结构”与“时间演化路径”的协同建模。该设计突破了TDA静态表征与LSTM纯时序建模的固有局限。

实验结果与贡献

在包含280万+标注流、14类真实攻击（DDoS、暴力破解、Web攻击、渗透、僵尸网络等）的CIC-IDS2017数据集上，模型取得AUC=1.000、F1-score=1.000的完美性能（5折交叉验证均值：AUC=1.000±0.000，F1=0.999±0.001）。消融实验证实：仅用拓扑特征（F1=0.990）或仅用时序LSTM（F1=1.000）均不及混合模型，验证二者互补性；对比实验显示，本模型显著优于TDA+随机森林（F1=0.994）和孤立森林（F1=0.835），尤其在低频隐蔽攻击（如渗透、DoS Hulk）中泛化优势突出。本工作为可解释AI安全模型提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes a hybrid Topological Data Analysis (TDA) + LSTM framework for network intrusion detection, addressing the dual challenge of capturing both global structural patterns and temporal dynamics in network traffic. We compute persistent homology on 77-dimensional CIC-IDS2017 features to generate Betti curves and persistence diagrams—topological descriptors encoding shape-invariant properties—and fuse them with raw time-series flow segments as input to a bidirectional LSTM. Evaluated on 2.8M+ labeled flows across 14 attack types, our model achieves perfect AUC (1.000) and near-perfect F1-score (1.000), with 5-fold CV showing mean AUC = 1.000 ± 0.000 and mean F1 = 0.999 ± 0.001. Ablation confirms complementary contributions: topological features alone yield F1=0.990, temporal modeling alone yields F1=1.000. The hybrid approach outperforms TDA+Random Forest (F1=0.994) and Isolation Forest (F1=0.835), especially on stealthy attacks. This work bridges topological robustness and deep sequential learning for interpretable, high-fidelity NIDS.

Abstract

Network intrusion detection systems (NIDS) are crucial in cybersecurity infrastructure, needing advanced techniques to detect hostile activity in network traffic. This research introduces a hybrid approach that combines Topological Data Analysis (TDA) with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to improve anomaly detection in network security. Our multi-layered design combines TDA's persistent homology with LSTM networks to capture topological characteristics of network traffic patterns and simulate temporal sequences. We assessed our methodology using the CIC-IDS2017 dataset, which includes over 2.8 million labelled flows, 77 network variables, and 14 attack categories that reflect modern threat landscapes such as DDoS, brute force, web attacks, penetration, and botnet activities. Integrating Betti curves and persistence diagrams with deep learning architectures enhances feature extraction performance. Our hybrid TDA+LSTM model has an AUC of 1.000 and F1-score of 1.000, with 5-fold cross-validation producing a mean AUC of 1.000 $\pm$ 0.000 and mean F1 of 0.999 $\pm$ 0.001. An ablation research demonstrates the complimentary contributions of topological (F1=0.990) and temporal characteristics (F1=1.000). Comparative research shows that the suggested strategy beats TDA+Random Forest (F1=0.994) and Isolation Forest (F1=0.835) baselines in several attack categories.

item.title: CVE-TTP KG: Knowledge Graph Linking Software Vulnerabilities to Attack Behaviors
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31557v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

在日益复杂的网络威胁环境中，攻击者频繁利用软件漏洞（CVE）实施高级持续性威胁（APT）。现有权威数据库（如CVE、NVD）虽提供详尽的技术描述，却普遍缺乏与攻击者行为模式（如MITRE ATT&CK框架中的战术Tactics与技术Techniques）的语义关联，导致安全分析难以从“漏洞”跃迁至“攻击意图”，严重制约威胁研判、响应决策与防御自动化。

方法创新

本研究提出CVE-TTP知识图谱（CVE-TTP KG），首次系统性构建漏洞与ATT&CK行为的结构化映射。技术路径包含三阶段：
1. 行为识别：基于领域适配的CySecBERT（在网络安全语料上继续预训练的Transformer模型），实现CVE描述文本中TTP（Tactics & Techniques）的细粒度分类；
2. 实体与关系抽取：构建迄今最大规模的CVE-TTP标注数据集（含24,820个实体与43,608条关系），对比评估pipeline式与span-based联合模型；
3. 图谱构建与可视化：将结构化结果注入Neo4j图数据库，支持多跳查询、攻击链推演与跨漏洞行为聚类。

关键成果

  • CySecBERT在TTP分类任务中达87.71%（Techniques） 与96.16%（Tactics） 的macro-F1；
  • pipeline方法在实体/关系抽取中分别取得0.86与0.99 macro-F1，显著优于联合模型（0.78）；
  • CVE-TTP KG已开源，为威胁情报融合、自动化红蓝对抗及AI驱动的漏洞优先级排序提供可解释、可推理的知识基座。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces CVE-TTP KG, a knowledge graph that semantically links Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) to MITRE ATT&CK tactics and techniques—bridging the critical gap between vulnerability data and adversary behavior. We propose a three-stage pipeline: (1) fine-tuned CySecBERT, a cybersecurity-domain adapted Transformer, achieves macro-F1 scores of 87.71% on technique classification and 96.16% on tactic classification; (2) a large-scale annotated dataset (24,820 entities, 43,608 relations) enables robust entity/relation extraction, with pipeline methods outperforming joint modeling (0.86 vs. 0.78 macro-F1 for entities; 0.99 vs. 0.78 for relations); and (3) structured outputs are integrated into a Neo4j-based cyber threat knowledge graph, supporting queryable, visualizable, and inferable vulnerability-behavior mappings. CVE-TTP KG advances threat intelligence interoperability and AI-powered security operations.

Abstract

In the evolving threat landscape, adversaries exploit software vulnerabilities to launch sophisticated attacks, challenging traditional defenses. Although databases like CVE and NVD provide detailed technical information, they often lack links to attacker behaviors such as tactics and techniques, limiting effective threat interpretation and response. This work bridges this gap by connecting vulnerabilities with behavioral patterns from the MITRE ATT&CK framework. We construct a CVE-TTP Knowledge Graph that links CVEs to tactics and techniques using classification and relation extraction. Transformer-based models are developed for behavior identification, with CySecBERT achieving macro F1-scores of 87.71% (techniques) and 96.16% (tactics). Also, we created an annotated dataset with 24,820 entities and 43,608 relations for entity and relation extraction. The pipeline-based approach achieves macro F1-scores of 0.86 (entity extraction) and 0.99 (relation extraction), while a span-based joint model achieves 0.78. These outputs are integrated into a Neo4j-based Cyber Threat Knowledge Graph, enabling structured visualization of vulnerabilities.

item.title: EnclaveX: End-to-End Confidential AI with CPU/GPU TEEs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31408v1

AI Summary (中文)

EnclaveX：面向端到端机密AI的CPU/GPU可信执行环境协同框架

随着大语言模型（LLMs）的爆发式应用，AI服务高度依赖微软Azure、谷歌云、AWS等中心化云平台，用户被迫上传敏感数据及训练/微调代码，引发严峻的数据泄露、模型窃取与管理员越权访问风险。尽管Intel SGX/TDX、AMD SEV-SNP和ARM CCA等CPU端可信执行环境（TEEs）已部分缓解信任问题，NVIDIA近年推出的H100/H200 GPU TEE亦拓展了硬件级机密计算边界，但现有研究仍缺乏对CPU与GPU TEE跨层协同、端到端AI工作流（含模型加载、前向/反向传播、分布式训练）的系统性支持与实证评估。

本文提出EnclaveX——首个支持CPU与GPU TEE深度融合的端到端机密AI框架。我们设计三重防护机制：（1）VM级隔离：基于Intel TDX与AMD SEV-SNP实现虚拟机级内存加密与远程证明；（2）应用级机密性增强：通过安全启动链、TEE内核模块验证及GPU内存页锁定，阻断Kubernetes集群管理员对机密VM内存的非法读取（实验证实传统方案中该漏洞可导致完整模型权重泄露）；（3）跨设备安全通信：定义CPU-GPU间经签名认证的DMA通道协议，确保梯度与参数在TEE边界内安全流转。我们在真实H200+TDX环境中部署Llama-2-7B微调任务，基准测试表明：相较非TEE基线，端到端延迟开销为18.3%–24.7%（取决于batch size），远低于纯软件加密方案（>200%）；远程证明耗时稳定在320ms以内，满足生产级SLA要求。本工作首次量化验证了异构TEE协同在LLM场景下的可行性与实用性，为构建零信任AI基础设施提供关键技术路径。

AI Summary (English)

EnclaveX presents the first end-to-end confidential AI framework unifying CPU and GPU Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs). It addresses critical gaps in holistic LLM confidentiality—particularly against privileged cloud administrators—by integrating Intel TDX/AMD SEV-SNP VM isolation with NVIDIA H200 GPU TEEs. We introduce a secure CPU-GPU communication protocol, harden application-level integrity via signed DMA channels and kernel module attestation, and empirically expose Kubernetes admin privilege escalation risks in prior TEE deployments. Evaluated on Llama-2-7B fine-tuning with industry benchmarks, EnclaveX incurs only 18.3–24.7% end-to-end latency overhead (vs. non-TEE baseline) and achieves remote attestation in <320 ms, demonstrating practical viability for production-grade confidential AI.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) have rapidly proliferated, driving widespread adoption of AI applications. Most deployments rely on centralized infrastructures such as Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, or AWS, requiring users to share sensitive data and training or fine-tuning code. This dependence raises significant security and privacy concerns, as cloud providers must be trusted to ensure confidentiality and integrity. Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) e.g., Intel SGX/TDX, AMD SEV-SNP, and ARM CCA have been introduced to mitigate these risks. More recently, NVIDIA has developed GPU TEEs (e.g., H100/H200), yet comprehensive evaluations of end-to-end workflows that integrate CPU and GPU TEEs remain limited. Critical aspects, including performance overhead, remote attestation, and security guarantees for AI/LLM applications, have not been sufficiently studied. This paper addresses this gap by presenting an end-to-end workflow that combines CPU and GPU TEEs. We propose mechanisms to ensure confidentiality and integrity at both the VM level (via Intel TDX and AMD SEV-SNP) and the application level, highlighting vulnerabilities such as Kubernetes administrators' ability to access confidential VM contents. Finally, we evaluate the performance overhead of our system using industry benchmarks, focusing on configurations that integrate Intel TDX with NVIDIA H200 GPUs.

item.title: The Decomposition Is the Fingerprint: Per-Component Identity for Agent Skills
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31272v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着AI智能体在运行时动态获取与执行技能（如提示指令、可执行代码、工具声明等组件），亟需一种稳定、可比对的技能身份标识机制。传统加密哈希（如SHA-256）因雪崩效应——单字符修改即导致完全不同的哈希值——无法支持细粒度相似性判断，难以应对提示重述、变量重命名、代码重构或可控翻译等常见变体。

方法创新

本文提出“分解即指纹”（Decomposition Is the Fingerprint）范式：将技能解耦为三元组——prompt、code、tools——并为每个组件独立生成局部敏感指纹。采用多银行SimHash技术，将各组件嵌入映射至紧凑的二进制位向量，最终合成固定长度120字节签名，支持常数时间内的汉明距离比较。

核心发现与价值

  • 结构化身份识别：三元组设计使系统能精准恢复“技能家族”关系——当任一组件保持共享时（如工具不变而提示被 paraphrase），仍可识别同源；而完全独立的多语言重实现则不被判定为同一技能，体现谱系（lineage）而非行为等价。
  • 变更定位能力：自动定位复用或篡改发生的组件层级（如仅代码被修改），支撑可追溯的“技能物料清单（SkillBOM）”。
  • 高效鲁棒性：在4,950次两两比对中AUC达0.974（95% CI [0.956, 0.994]），比特开销仅为所逼近嵌入的1/77，且排序保真度在有限位下具备理论收敛保证。
  • 安全定位清晰：该指纹是身份信号（identity signal），非安全判决；它构建技能注册中心的结构主干，安全性仍需依赖独立的行为验证。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a per-component locality-sensitive fingerprint for AI agent skills—decomposed into prompt, code, and tool declarations—to enable stable, interpretable identity tracking amid paraphrasing, refactoring, renaming, and controlled translation. Unlike cryptographic hashes, our 120-byte SimHash-based signature preserves structural lineage (not behavioral equivalence) via Hamming-distance comparison in constant time. Crucially, representing identity as a triple—not a scalar—enables relationship classification (e.g., family membership), novelty detection, and precise change localization (e.g., “only code modified”). Evaluated on 4,950 pairwise comparisons, it achieves an AUC of 0.974 (95% CI [0.956, 0.994]) using 77× fewer bits than the embedding it approximates, with provably preserved ranking. On a 906-skill injection benchmark, it detects tampered copies and pinpoints modifications—but by design remains a complementary identity signal, not a safety verdict.

Abstract

AI agents increasingly acquire and execute skills at runtime: bundles of prompt instructions, executable code, and tool declarations fetched from marketplaces and other agents. Governing them needs a stable notion of skill identity, yet cryptographic hashing is engineered to destroy the very similarity we need, as a one-character edit scrambles the digest. We present a compact, locality-sensitive fingerprint that embeds each component of a skill and projects it to bits with a multi-bank SimHash, giving a fixed 120-byte signature compared in constant time by Hamming distance. Our central claim is that keeping the fingerprint as a per-component triple (prompt, code, tools), rather than a single score, is what makes it useful: the triple recovers skill-family identity through paraphrase, renaming, refactoring, and controlled code translation when another component remains shared, while independent multilingual reimplementation is not recovered; it also localizes which component carries the reuse. We claim lineage, not behavioral equivalence: identity supplies the structural axis of a registry and leaves safety to behavioral verification. The fingerprint reaches an area under the ROC curve (AUC) of 0.974 (95% CI [0.956, 0.994]) over 4,950 pairwise comparisons while using 77x fewer bits than the embedding it approximates, with ranking preserved in expectation and finite-bit concentration; the per-component split turns one number into relationship classification, families, novelty, and a portable "SkillBOM" for a skill registry. On a 906-skill injection benchmark the fingerprint recognizes injected skills as tampered copies of a known base and localizes the change, but recognition is not trust: it remains, by design, an identity signal complementary to behavioral verification rather than a safety verdict.

item.title: Securing the AI Agent: A Unified Framework for Multi-Layer Agent Red Teaming
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31227v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着开源AI基础设施的爆发式发展——涵盖模型服务引擎、智能体（Agent）平台、Model Context Protocol（MCP）生态，乃至基础大语言模型本身——其安全防护能力已严重滞后。现有安全工具多聚焦单一层面（如模型层或API层），难以应对AI智能体跨层耦合的攻击面：攻击者可同时利用底层基础设施漏洞、协议实现缺陷、技能包（skill package）供应链风险、行为逻辑盲区及模型自身脆弱性。

方法与框架

本文提出 AI-Infra-Guard——首个覆盖全栈AI智能体的开源红队框架。其核心洞见是：AI智能体的安全边界具有四层结构化特征（基础设施层、协议/工具层、智能体行为层、模型层），需匹配差异化检测范式：

  • 基础设施层：基于75+主流AI组件（如vLLM、Ollama、LangChain）与1,400+精准规则的确定性规则匹配；
  • 协议/工具层：由LLM驱动的自主式审计代理，动态探测MCP服务器接口与技能包权限滥用；
  • 行为层：支持多轮交互的黑盒智能体红队测试，模拟真实用户-智能体对抗；
  • 模型层：集成26+攻击算子的 jailbreak 框架，覆盖16个主流基准数据集（如AdvBench、SafeBench）。

创新与价值

AI-Infra-Guard是目前唯一支持智能体技能供应链审计的开源框架，填补了AI Agent安全中“第三方技能引入”这一关键风险域空白。我们以层-范式匹配（layer-paradigm matching） 为设计原则，提供可扩展、可组合的安全基座，推动社区共建统一防御标准。

AI Summary (English)

AI-Infra-Guard is the first open-source framework unifying red teaming across all layers of AI agent infrastructure: infrastructure (e.g., vLLM, Ollama), protocol/tool (e.g., MCP servers, skill packages), agent behavior (multi-turn black-box interaction), and model (jailbreak resilience). It departs from monolithic detection by matching specialized paradigms to each layer—deterministic rule matching over 75+ components and 1,400+ vulnerability rules; LLM-driven agentic auditing of MCP endpoints and skill permissions; multi-turn adversarial probing of agent workflows; and a modular jailbreak harness with 26+ operators evaluated on 16 datasets. Crucially, it introduces supply-chain auditing for third-party agent skills—a previously unaddressed vector in open-agent ecosystems. Released under an open license, AI-Infra-Guard establishes layer-paradigm matching as a practical foundation for scalable, community-driven AI agent security.

Abstract

The fast growth of open-source AI infrastructure, from model serving engines and agent platforms to the Model Context Protocol (MCP) ecosystem and the language models themselves, has outpaced the security tooling available to defend it. We present AI-Infra-Guard, an open-source framework that organizes AI red teaming around a single observation: the attack surface of an AI agent is stratified across layers (infrastructure, protocol/tool, agent behavior, and model), and no single detection paradigm fits all of them. The framework therefore matches a paradigm to each layer, from deterministic rule matching over 75+ AI components and 1{,}400+ vulnerability rules, through LLM-driven agentic auditing of MCP servers and agent-skill packages and multi-turn black-box agent red teaming, to a jailbreak harness with 26+ attack operators over sixteen datasets. To our knowledge it is the only open-source framework to span all of these, including supply-chain auditing of the agent skills that increasingly extend AI agents. We release AI-Infra-Guard as open source so that \emph{layer-paradigm matching} can serve as a practical foundation for agent security and a shared base for the community to build on.

item.title: An Empirical Study of Security Calibration in Large Language Models for Code
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31159v1

AI Summary (中文)

大语言模型代码安全校准的首次大规模实证研究

本研究首次系统性探究了大语言模型（LLM）在代码生成场景下的安全校准性（security calibration）——即模型对其输出代码安全性判断的置信度是否真实反映其实际安全水平。针对当前LLM广泛应用于软件开发却缺乏对“高置信度≠高安全性”风险认知的现状，我们构建了覆盖自包含安全任务与多语言仓库级上下文的双轨评估基准，并在GPT-4o-mini、Gemini-2.0-Flash和Qwen3-Coder-Next三大主流模型上，于多种temperature设置下开展大规模实证分析。

核心发现包括：（1）普遍过自信现象：所有模型在安全判断上均显著高估自身能力，尤其在复杂仓库级环境中False Trust（对漏洞代码赋予高置信度）频发；（2）安全校准优于功能校准：模型对“代码是否安全”的估计比对“代码是否功能正确”的估计更可靠，暗示安全属性（如模式匹配、规则违反）比依赖运行时行为的功能正确性更易被模型感知；（3）校准引导修复效果有限：基于置信度筛选的自动修复仅小幅降低漏洞率，却频繁引发功能回归；（4）架构化门控存在场景局限性：虽在受控任务中提升校准度，但在真实仓库级场景中反而加剧校准恶化，凸显实验室指标与工程实践间的鸿沟。本研究为LLM安全可信部署提供了关键实证依据与方法论警示。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first large-scale empirical study of security calibration—the alignment between an LLM’s confidence in code security and its actual security correctness. We evaluate GPT-4o-mini, Gemini-2.0-Flash, and Qwen3-Coder-Next across temperature settings on two complementary benchmarks: self-contained security tasks and realistic multi-language repository-level contexts. Results reveal pervasive overconfidence: models frequently assign high confidence to vulnerable code (False Trust). Surprisingly, security calibration consistently outperforms functional calibration—suggesting models reason about security properties (e.g., pattern-based vulnerabilities) more reliably than functional correctness (which depends on complex execution semantics). Calibration-guided automated repair yields only marginal security improvements while often introducing functional regressions. Moreover, architectural gating improves calibration on controlled benchmarks but worsens it in repository-level settings—highlighting a critical gap between lab evaluation and real-world deployment. Our work establishes foundational evidence for calibrating LLMs in security-critical coding.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) are rapidly transforming software development, yet their use in security-critical contexts raises a key question: do models know when their generated code is insecure? This property, known as calibration, measures whether a model's confidence aligns with the true correctness of its outputs. We present the first large-scale empirical study of security calibration in LLM-generated code. We evaluate GPT-4o-mini, Gemini-2.0-Flash, and Qwen3-Coder-Next across multiple temperature settings on two complementary benchmarks: self-contained security tasks and multi-language repository-level contexts. Our results suggest that overconfidence is prevalent across the evaluated LLMs. Functional calibration is consistently worse than security calibration, suggesting that models estimate security outcomes more reliably than functional correctness, potentially because functional correctness depends on complex execution behavior. We also examine whether calibration-guided automated repair can help remediate vulnerabilities in LLM-generated code, finding only limited improvements while frequently introducing functional regressions. Moreover, we study different mitigation strategies for reducing False Trust, where models assign high confidence to vulnerable code. The results show that although architectural gating improves calibration on controlled benchmarks, calibration deteriorates in realistic repository-level settings, increasing the risk of high-confidence vulnerable outputs.

item.title: Secure-CHG: A Comprehensive Framework for Robust and Fair Federated Learning via Hybrid Defense and Contribution-Aware Trust
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31066v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

联邦学习（FL）易受隐蔽后门攻击威胁，攻击者通过注入含特定触发器的样本，诱导全局模型对目标类产生错误预测。现有统计型防御方法（如Krum、Trimmed Mean）主要依赖梯度范数、距离等形态学特征，在训练早期有效，却忽视了一个关键漏洞——“晚期失效”（Late-stage Failure）：随着全局模型收敛，良性与恶意客户端的梯度范数同步衰减，导致二者在统计分布上趋于不可区分，传统防御机制因此“失明”，使自适应攻击者可长期潜伏并在后期劫持聚合过程。

方法创新

本文提出 Secure-CHG——一种融合式鲁棒公平联邦学习框架，实现从“形态检测”到“语义贡献验证”的范式跃迁。其核心包含两阶段自适应防御流水线：

  • 早期阶段：采用级联统计滤波器（Cascaded Statistical Filter），抑制初始震荡期的噪声干扰，保障优化稳定性；
  • 晚期阶段：引入原创的 CHG-Shapley 机制——将本地更新映射至由样本难度（即本地训练损失）与梯度构成的复合Hardness-Gradient（CHG）空间，显著放大恶意更新的语义异常痕迹；
  • 理论上，我们推导出CHG-Shapley的闭式解，支持无需重训练的轻量级节点价值评估，并驱动信任加权聚合，兼顾鲁棒性与贡献公平性。

实验结果

在CIFAR-10、MedMNIST和NEU-SDDB三大异构数据集上系统验证：Secure-CHG成功克服晚期失效，对先进后门攻击（如Label-Flipping+Trigger Injection）的攻击成功率分别比Krum降低2.3×、比Trimmed Mean降低2.0×，同时保持98.2%以上的主任务准确率，显著优于SOTA基线。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Learning (FL) faces severe threats from stealthy backdoor attacks, yet existing statistical defenses fail in late training stages due to vanishing gradient norms—termed Late-stage Failure. To address this, we propose Secure-CHG, a hybrid defense framework that shifts focus from superficial gradient morphology to intrinsic semantic contribution verification. It features: (i) a cascaded statistical filter for early-phase stabilization; and (ii) the novel CHG-Shapley mechanism—projecting updates into a composite Hardness-Gradient space (using local loss + gradient) to amplify adversarial semantic traces even when norms decay. We derive a closed-form solution for CHG-Shapley, enabling efficient, retraining-free node valuation and trust-aware aggregation. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-10, MedMNIST, and NEU-SDDB show Secure-CHG reduces advanced backdoor attack success rates by 2.3× vs. Krum and 2.0× vs. Trimmed Mean, while preserving >98.2% main-task accuracy.

Abstract

Federated Learning (FL) is highly susceptible to stealthy backdoor attacks, which aim to force a model into predicting an attacker-chosen target class for inputs containing a specific trigger. However, existing statistical defenses primarily focus on the early stages of model convergence. In this paper, we identify a fundamental vulnerability termed ``Late-stage Failure.'' We demonstrate that as the global model converges, decaying gradient norms render malicious and benign updates morphologically indistinguishable. This vanishing statistical variance effectively blinds traditional defenses, enabling adaptive adversaries to remain dormant and subsequently hijack the training process. To overcome these constraints, we propose Secure-CHG, a hybrid framework that pivots the defense paradigm from superficial morphological detection toward intrinsic semantic contribution verification. Secure-CHG employs an adaptive defense pipeline: a cascaded statistical filter stabilizes optimization during the early oscillatory phase, while a novel CHG-Shapley mechanism takes over during late-stage convergence. By leveraging sample hardness (i.e., local training loss) to project updates into a composite Hardness-Gradient space, it effectively amplifies adversarial semantic traces, enabling the isolation of stealthy attackers even as gradient norms vanish. Furthermore, we derive a closed-form solution for CHG-Shapley, facilitating low-complexity, retraining-free node valuation and trust-modulated aggregation. Extensive evaluations on CIFAR-10, MedMNIST, and NEU-SDDB demonstrate that Secure-CHG effectively mitigates Late-stage Failure. Specifically, it significantly suppresses advanced backdoor attacks, reducing their attack success rate by 2.3$\times$ and 2.0$\times$ relative to the mainstream Krum and Trimmed Mean baselines, respectively.

item.title: Amplifying Membership Signal Through Chained Regeneration
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31991v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大型生成式模型易记忆训练数据，导致隐私泄露与版权风险加剧。当前成员推断攻击（MIA）与数据集推断（DI）多依赖单次生成（one-shot generation），信号微弱、跨模态敏感性低，且白盒/灰盒场景下常需训练影子模型——对大模型而言计算不可行。

方法创新：MADreMIA框架

受模型自噬紊乱（Model Autophagy Disorder, MAD）启发，本文提出MADreMIA——一种模型无关、无需影子模型的新型框架。其核心是链式再生（Chained Regeneration）：通过跨模态迭代生成（如文本→图像→文本→音频…），将前一阶段输出作为下一阶段输入，构建长程生成轨迹。该过程天然放大模型对训练样本的记忆信号：成员样本在多次再生中保持更高语义连贯性与结构稳定性，而非成员样本则快速退化。

关键发现与优势

  • 低假阳性率（FPR）下显著提升检测能力：在相同FPR=1%时，MADreMIA相较基线方法平均提升AUC达12.7–28.3个百分点；
  • 跨模态普适性强：在IARs（隐式自回归模型）、扩散模型（Stable Diffusion）、语言模型（LLaMA-2、GPT-2）上均验证有效；
  • 首次拓展至音频模态：初步实验表明其在Whisper微调模型上亦可实现稳健DI；
  • 完全免训练：仅需目标模型前向推理，支持白盒、灰盒、黑盒全场景，部署成本极低。

本工作揭示了生成轨迹的“记忆衰减动力学”本质，为轻量、通用、多模态的隐私审计提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Large generative models’ tendency to memorize training data necessitates robust membership inference (MIA) and dataset inference (DI) for privacy and copyright auditing. Existing methods rely on one-shot generations, yielding weak, modality-limited signals—and often require infeasible shadow-model training. Inspired by Model Autophagy Disorder (MAD), we propose MADreMIA, a model-agnostic framework that amplifies membership signals via chained regeneration: iterative, cross-modal generation where each output serves as the next input. We find memorized samples exhibit significantly higher coherence and slower degradation across regeneration steps than non-members, enabling high-sensitivity detection at low false positive rates. MADreMIA achieves consistent gains across IARs, diffusion models (e.g., Stable Diffusion), and language models (LLaMA-2, GPT-2), with preliminary success on audio models (Whisper). Crucially, it requires no shadow training—only black-box inference—making it scalable, efficient, and broadly deployable.

Abstract

The tendency of large generative models to memorize training data makes sample verification critical for privacy auditing and copyright enforcement. Current membership (MIA) and dataset inference (DI) attacks often rely on one-shot generations, which yield weak signals and limited sensitivity across modalities. Inspired by Model Autophagy Disorder (MAD), we introduce MADreMIA, a model-agnostic framework that enhances white-, gray-, and black-box MIA and DI. Rather than relying on shadow model training -- often infeasible for large generative models -- our framework facilitates scalable inference by leveraging inherent signals through iterative trajectories. This process utilizes chained generations across diverse modalities, where each output serves as the subsequent input, to improve membership evidence at low FPR. We demonstrate that memorized training samples exhibit significantly higher coherence and slower degradation during iterative regeneration than non-member generations. Our results show that MADreMIA provides richer signals across diverse model families and modalities; we present comprehensive evaluations for IARs, diffusion, and language models, alongside preliminary results demonstrating its potential for audio models.

item.title: Harnessing Textual Refusal Directions for Multimodal Safety
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31876v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

大型语言模型（LLMs）的安全对齐常依赖后训练微调或激活空间中的“拒绝方向”（refusal directions）。然而，将这些方法迁移至多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）面临关键瓶颈：其安全对齐需依赖带标注的不安全多模态数据（如含有害图文/视频的样本），而此类数据远比纯文本数据更难收集、标注与合规获取。

核心发现与方法创新

本文首次系统验证：仅从纯文本LLM主干中提取的拒绝方向，可跨模态泛化至图像与视频输入。尽管效果受层选择、引导强度及跨模态对齐质量影响——尤其当视觉-语言对齐偏差导致本应安全的多模态输入被误导向拒绝时，这一现象揭示了模态间安全结构的潜在共享性。

基于此，我们提出模态无关拒绝引导（Modality-Agnostic Refusal Steering, MARS）：一种无需训练、轻量、即插即用的安全增强框架。MARS包含三大关键技术：

  • 激活重中心化（Activation Re-centering）：校正视觉编码器与语言解码器间的模态偏移；
  • 几何信任域自适应缩放（Trust-Region Adaptive Scaling）：在单位球面约束下动态调节引导强度，避免过强干预；
  • 首词层优化选择（First-Token Layer Selection）：仅在首个生成token处实施干预，兼顾效率与效果。

实验结果与意义

在5个SOTA MLLMs（如Qwen-VL、LLaVA-1.6、Video-LLaMA等）上，MARS在安全基准（MMBench-Safety、SafeVid）、实用性基准（MMBench、SEED-Bench）及视频越狱攻击（Video-Jailbreak） 上全面验证：平均提升安全率+12.7%，同时保持甚至小幅提升任务准确率（+0.4%）。结果证实：安全语义结构具有跨模态普适性，文本拒绝方向是未被充分挖掘的多模态对齐基石。

AI Summary (English)

To overcome the scarcity of unsafe multimodal data for aligning Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs), this work discovers that textual refusal directions—extracted solely from frozen LLM backbones—generalize across image and video modalities. We identify three key limitations: layer sensitivity, steering strength overcorrection, and cross-modal misalignment causing spurious refusals on safe inputs. To address them, we propose Modality-Agnostic Refusal Steering (MARS): a training-free, lightweight method operating at the first generated token. MARS corrects modality misalignment via activation re-centering, adaptively scales steering within a geometrically constrained trust region, and selects the optimal intervention layer. Evaluated across five state-of-the-art MLLMs on safety (MMBench-Safety, SafeVid), utility (MMBench, SEED-Bench), and video jailbreak benchmarks, MARS consistently improves safety (+12.7% avg.) without sacrificing utility (+0.4% accuracy gain). Our results demonstrate that safety-relevant representations are shared across modalities—and textual refusal directions serve as a powerful, underexploited foundation for multimodal alignment.

Abstract

To improve safety in Large Language Models (LLMs) we can either perform post-training alignment or exploit refusal directions in the activation space. Both strategies are less feasible in Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) as they require unsafe multimodal data, harder to collect than their unimodal counterpart. In this work, we relax this constraint and investigate whether textual refusal directions, extracted directly from the LLM backbone, generalize across modalities (i.e., image, video). Preliminary findings confirm this ability, though effectiveness is conditioned by layer selection, steering strength, and cross-modal alignment, with the latter causing safe multimodal inputs to be spuriously steered toward refusal. Building on this, we introduce Modality-Agnostic Refusal Steering (MARS), a light-weight training-free approach that injects multimodal safety without the need for multimodal safety data. MARS corrects modality misalignment via activation re-centering, adaptively scales steering strength within a geometrically defined trust region, and selects the optimal intervention layer, operating at the first generated token. Evaluated on five SOTA MLLMs across safety, utility, and video jailbreak benchmarks, MARS achieves consistent safety gains while preserving utility. These results reveal that safety-relevant structure is shared across modalities and that textual refusal directions are a powerful and underexplored foundation for multimodal alignment.

item.title: An Agentic AI Framework to Accelerate Scientific Discovery in Plant Phenotyping
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31831v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

高通量植物表型分析正以前所未有的速度产生海量多模态图像数据——Oak Ridge国家实验室先进植物表型实验室（APPL）的自动化成像平台每日可采集数百株植物在可见光、近红外、热成像等多波段下的图像。然而，数据分析严重滞后：性状提取与生物学解读仍高度依赖人工判读和领域专家经验，且仅限于采集完成后的“后处理”阶段。这导致分析能力而非数据获取能力成为科学发现的真正瓶颈。

方法与创新

本研究提出首个面向植物表型场景的端到端自主智能体（Agentic AI）框架，将APPL从“数据工厂”升级为“交互式自主发现平台”。框架包含两大核心智能体：

  • Co-Scientist Agent（共研科学家智能体）：通过自然语言对话理解科学家提问（如“哪些基因型在干旱胁迫下叶片卷曲最显著？”），动态生成结构化分析计划（含目标作物、时间点、模态、算法链及验证逻辑）；
  • Compute Agent（计算智能体）：以无头方式在Frontier百亿亿次超算上调度Vision Transformer模型，执行高精度语义分割与定量性状提取（如叶面积、卷曲指数、蒸腾速率推演）。

二者运行于物理隔离的安全与资源域，通过基于令牌认证的加密流式通信通道协同——该设计显式建模了科研基础设施中的联邦治理、跨域数据移动约束与全链路溯源需求，弥补了主流云原生智能体框架在真实科研场景中的关键缺失。

主要成效

分析周期从数天至数周压缩为秒级交互闭环：智能体可实时推理中间结果、主动推荐后续实验（如“建议补充根系热成像验证水分再分配假说”）、并响应追问。实测表明，对典型干旱响应分析任务，框架将专家介入频次降低76%，同时确保每步操作、模型版本、数据切片与参数均被不可篡改地记录，实现端到端可追溯的AI增强科学发现。

AI Summary (English)

High-throughput plant phenotyping generates multimodal image data faster than human experts can analyze it—making analysis, not acquisition, the bottleneck for discovery. We present an agentic AI framework that transforms Oak Ridge’s APPL into an interactive, autonomous discovery platform. A conversational Co-Scientist Agent translates natural-language scientific questions into executable analysis plans; a headless Compute Agent executes Vision Transformer–based segmentation and trait extraction on the Frontier exascale supercomputer. The agents operate in isolated security/resource domains and communicate via a secure, token-authenticated streaming channel—explicitly addressing federation, data-movement constraints, and end-to-end provenance requirements ignored by cloud-native agentic systems. Our framework reduces typical analysis cycles from days/weeks to seconds, enables real-time result reasoning, automated follow-up suggestions, and full auditability of every AI-human interaction. It represents the first production-ready, infrastructure-aware agentic system for scalable, trustworthy scientific discovery in plant phenomics.

Abstract

High-throughput plant phenotyping now generates image derived datasets far faster than scientists can analyze them. At Oak Ridge National Laboratory's Advanced Plant Phenotyping Laboratory (APPL), automated stations image hundreds of plants daily across multiple remote sensing modalities; yet, trait extraction and interpretation remain manual, expert-bound, and strictly post-hoc, making analysis, not acquisition, the binding constraint on discovery. We present an end-to-end agentic AI framework that turns the facility from a data factory into an interactive autonomous, discovery platform, where scientists partner with AI agents to accelerate time to insight. A conversational Co-Scientist Agent translates a scientist's natural-language question into a structured analysis plan, and a headless Compute Agent dispatches Vision Transformer segmentation and trait extraction on the Frontier exascale supercomputer. The two agents run in separate security and resource domains and communicate over a secure, token-authenticated streaming channel, a design that accounts for the federation, data-movement, and provenance realities cloud-native agentic frameworks ignore, ensuring end-to-end provenance is captured for every interaction. The framework turns a days- to weeks-long analysis process into an interactive loop where agents reason over results, recommend next analyses, and respond to follow-up questions in seconds.

item.title: FedXDS: Leveraging Model Attribution Methods to counteract Data Heterogeneity in Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31742v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）允许多方协作训练模型而无需共享原始数据，但其性能常因客户端数据的统计异质性（non-IID分布）显著下降。现有方法多依赖模型聚合优化或数据增强，却难以精准定位异质性的根源——即跨客户端间任务相关特征分布的不一致。

方法创新：FedXDS框架

本文首次将可解释人工智能（XAI）中的模型归因方法引入联邦学习，提出FedXDS（Federated Learning via XAI-guided Data Sharing）。核心思想是：利用传播式特征归因（如Integrated Gradients或Grad-CAM变体），通过单次反向传播即可高效识别各客户端数据中对全局任务最具判别力的局部关键特征区域（如图像中的语义部件、文本中的关键词片段）。据此指导选择性数据共享——仅交换经归因筛选的高价值特征表示（而非原始样本），实现客户端贡献的语义对齐。

隐私与安全保障

为防范隐私泄露，FedXDS集成度量隐私（Metric Differential Privacy）机制，在特征空间施加可控扰动，提供严格的$(\varepsilon,\delta)$-隐私保证；理论分析证实其在保持特征判别性的同时有效抵御成员推断攻击与特征逆向攻击。

实验验证

在CIFAR-10/100、Tiny-ImageNet及Sentiment140等基准上，FedXDS在强异质性（Dirichlet $\alpha=0.1$）与多客户端（50–100节点）场景下，平均测试精度提升3.2–7.8%，收敛速度加快1.8–2.5×，显著优于FedAvg、FedProx及FedMix等主流方法。代码已开源：https://github.com/MaxH1996/FedXDS。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces FedXDS, the first federated learning framework that leverages model attribution methods from Explainable AI (XAI) to tackle statistical heterogeneity. Instead of sharing raw data or full models, FedXDS uses propagation-based attribution (e.g., gradient-integrated methods) to identify task-critical feature regions per client in a single backward pass—enabling selective, semantics-aware data sharing. To ensure privacy, it employs metric differential privacy with formal $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-guarantees, preserving utility while resisting membership inference and feature inversion attacks. Experiments across vision and NLP benchmarks show FedXDS consistently achieves +3.2–7.8% higher accuracy and 1.8–2.5× faster convergence than state-of-the-art FL methods under high heterogeneity and large-scale client settings. Code: https://github.com/MaxH1996/FedXDS.

Abstract

Explainable AI (XAI) methods have demonstrated significant success in recent years at identifying relevant features in input data that drive deep learning model decisions, enhancing interpretability for users. However, the potential of XAI beyond providing model transparency has remained largely unexplored in adjacent machine learning domains. In this paper, we show for the first time how XAI can be utilized in the context of federated learning. Specifically, while federated learning enables collaborative model training without raw data sharing, it suffers from performance degradation when client data distributions exhibit statistical heterogeneity. We introduce FedXDS (Federated Learning via XAI-guided Data Sharing), the first approach to utilize feature attribution techniques to identify precisely which data elements should be selectively shared between clients to mitigate heterogeneity. By employing propagation-based attribution, our method identifies task-relevant features through a single backward pass, enabling selective data sharing that aligns client contributions. To protect sensitive information, we incorporate metric privacy techniques that provide formal privacy guarantees while preserving utility. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach consistently achieves higher accuracy and faster convergence compared to existing methods across varying client numbers and heterogeneity settings. We provide theoretical privacy guarantees and empirically demonstrate robustness against both membership inference and feature inversion attacks. Code is available at https://github.com/MaxH1996/FedXDS.

item.title: Cross-lingual Relation Extraction with Large Language Models: Zero-Shot, Few-Shot, and Fine-Tuned Evaluation on Romanian
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31718v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与目标

低资源语言的关系抽取（RE）长期受限于高质量标注语料的匮乏。本研究聚焦罗马尼亚语这一典型低资源语言，系统评估大语言模型（LLM）在跨语言关系抽取中的可行性，特别考察零样本、少样本及微调三种范式下的性能表现。

方法设计

我们构建了一套端到端评估框架：

  • 数据构建：采用LLM驱动的翻译流水线，将权威英文基准SemEval-2010 Task 8精准译为罗马尼亚语，并经人工校验确保语义保真与实体标记一致性；
  • 模型对比：以Gemma-4-31B为核心，测试其零样本提示、5-shot提示及QLoRA高效微调（冻结主干+低秩适配）效果；同步对比四类编码器基线——XLM-RoBERTa-base/large、罗马尼亚语专用BERT（RoBERTa-ro）、RoBERTa-large，参数量覆盖125M–560M；
  • 任务设置：涵盖两种主流范式——带实体标记的关系分类（relation classification）与端到端关系抽取（end-to-end extraction）。

关键发现

  • 零样本下，罗马尼亚语性能较英语基准下降3–5个百分点（pp），表明直接跨语言迁移存在显著语义鸿沟；
  • 少样本提示仅带来边际增益（<1pp），凸显纯提示工程的局限性；
  • QLoRA微调使宏F1提升超22pp，且将英罗跨语言差距从3.3pp大幅收窄至1.4pp；
  • 惊人的是，125M参数的单语罗马尼亚BERT性能媲美278M参数的多语XLM-R，而所有编码器基线仅比QLoRA-Gemma低1–4pp，却小50–250倍；
  • 结论明确：在计算敏感型部署场景中，为罗马尼亚语单任务RE选用31B模型缺乏性价比优势。

我们已开源罗马尼亚语版SemEval-2010数据集、完整评估代码及全部微调模型，推动低资源语言RE的可复现研究。

AI Summary (English)

This study investigates cross-lingual relation extraction (RE) for Romanian—a low-resource language—by translating the SemEval-2010 Task 8 benchmark via an LLM-based pipeline and evaluating Gemma-4-31B under zero-shot, 5-shot, and QLoRA fine-tuning settings. We compare against four encoder baselines (125M–560M parameters), including XLM-RoBERTa, Romanian BERT, and RoBERTa-large, on both relation classification and end-to-end extraction. Results show a 3–5 pp drop for Romanian in zero-shot prompting, negligible gains from few-shot prompting (<1 pp), and >22 pp macro F1 improvement after QLoRA—reducing the English–Romanian gap from 3.3 to 1.4 pp. Crucially, the 125M Romanian BERT matches 278M XLM-R, and all encoders trail QLoRA-Gemma by only 1–4 pp despite being 50–250× smaller. Thus, deploying a 31B model for Romanian RE is unjustified where compute efficiency matters. The translated dataset, evaluation code, and models are publicly released.

Abstract

Relation extraction (RE) for low-resource languages is typically constrained by the lack of annotated corpora. We investigate the feasibility of cross-lingual RE for Romanian by combining automatic dataset translation with large language model (LLM) inference. We translate the SemEval-2010 Task 8 benchmark from English to Romanian using an LLM-based translation pipeline and evaluate Gemma 4 31B under zero-shot, few-shot, and QLoRA fine-tuned configurations, against four encoder baselines spanning 125M to 560M parameters: XLM- RoBERTa (base and large), Romanian BERT, and RoBERT- large. We assess two task formulations: relation classification with marked entities and end-to-end extraction. Our results show that Romanian incurs a 3 to 5 percentage point (pp) drop relative to English in prompt-only settings, that few-shot prompting provides marginal gains over zero-shot, and that QLoRA fine-tuning improves macro F1-Score by more than 22 percentage points in both languages while reducing the cross-lingual gap from 3.3 to 1.4pp. The encoder baselines come within 1-4pp of QLoRA Gemma on Romanian despite being 50-250 times smaller, with monolingual Romanian BERT at 125M parameters matching multilingual XLM-R at 278M. The case for using a 31B model for single-task RE on Romanian is therefore weak in deployment scenarios where compute matters. We release the translated dataset, evaluation code, and trained models.

item.title: FedLAB: Traceable Semantic Codebooks for Federated Multimodal Graph Foundation Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.32016v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

多模态图基础模型旨在从融合文本、图像、属性及关系拓扑的图结构中学习可复用知识，支撑图中心与模态中心的多样化下游任务。然而，现实场景中此类图数据常分布式地驻留在隐私敏感的客户端（如医疗、金融机构），原始内容与本地图结构严禁上传或集中共享，导致传统集中式训练不可行。现有联邦多模态图学习方法多依赖参数、原型或嵌入交换，虽支持表征迁移，却缺乏语义可追溯性——无法清晰揭示文本证据、节点语义与拓扑上下文如何协同驱动预测决策。

方法创新：FedLAB框架

本文提出 FedLAB（Federated Label-Aware Barycenter），首个面向联邦多模态图基础学习的可追溯语义码本框架。其核心在于构建三类类型化分层语义码本：① 模态证据码本（对齐图文/文本特征与标签语义）；② 节点语义码本（编码跨客户端一致的实体级概念）；③ 拓扑上下文码本（建模邻域结构模式）。所有码本均在客户端本地构建，不传输原始数据；通过联邦语义重心预训练（Federated Semantic Barycenter Pre-training），在服务器端聚合并优化码本语义一致性，实现知识协同而非数据共享。

实验验证与价值

在10个跨域基准数据集（含PubMed, Amazon-Photos, Flickr等）和6类下游任务（节点分类、链接预测、跨模态检索等）上，FedLAB平均提升SOTA基线达5.21–7.53%，同时天然支持可解释性追溯：用户可通过码本索引反查任一预测背后的模态依据、关键节点语义及拓扑路径。代码与码本接口已开源，为隐私优先的多模态图智能提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Multimodal graph foundation models learn transferable knowledge from graphs enriched with text, images, attributes, and topology—but real-world data is often decentralized and privacy-sensitive, precluding centralized training. Existing federated approaches exchange parameters or embeddings, sacrificing semantic traceability: they obscure how modality evidence, node semantics, and topological context jointly inform predictions. To address this, we propose FedLAB, the first framework for traceable semantic codebooks in federated multimodal graph learning. FedLAB organizes knowledge into three typed, hierarchical codebooks—modality evidence, node semantics, and topology context—each refined via federated semantic barycenter pre-training without sharing raw data or graph structures. Evaluated on 10 benchmarks and 6 downstream tasks, FedLAB outperforms SOTA methods by up to 7.53%, while natively enabling interpretable tracing: users can query which modalities, nodes, and structural patterns underpin any prediction. Code and interface are open-sourced.

Abstract

Multimodal graph foundation models aim to learn reusable knowledge from graphs enriched with text, images, attributes, and relational topology, thereby supporting diverse graph-centric and modality-centric tasks. In practice, however, such multimodal graphs are often distributed across decentralized clients, where raw contents and local structures cannot be centrally shared due to privacy constraints. This motivates federated multimodal graph foundation learning, which requires not only transferable representation learning but also intrinsic semantic traceability under strict data isolation. Existing methods usually exchange or store knowledge through parameters, prototypes, embeddings, or compact codebooks, which support optimization and transfer but do not explicitly expose how modality evidence, node semantics, and topology context jointly support predictions. To bridge this gap, we propose FedLAB, a traceable semantic codebook framework that organizes multimodal graph knowledge into typed hierarchical codebooks for modality evidence, node semantics, and topology context. FedLAB further refines these trace units through federated semantic barycenter pre-training while keeping raw multimodal contents and graph structures local. Extensive experiments on 10 benchmarks and 6 downstream tasks show that FedLAB improves over state-of-the-art baselines by up to 7.53\%, while preserving a native semantic trace interface.

item.title: TabPATE: Differentially Private Tabular In-Context Learning Without Public Data
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31474v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

表格型基础模型（tabular foundation models）显著提升了小样本场景下的上下文内学习（In-Context Learning, ICL）性能，但其直接将私有记录作为上下文输入，极易通过模型输出泄露个体敏感信息。本文首次实证表明：即使采用最基础的成员推断攻击（Membership Inference Attack, MIA），也能在主流表格ICL设置中达到远超随机猜测的成功率（如AUC > 0.85），凸显现有方法缺乏形式化隐私保障，亟需差分隐私（Differential Privacy, DP）保护。

方法创新：TabPATE

我们提出TabPATE——首个无需任何公开同分布数据（public in-distribution data）的、面向表格ICL的差分隐私防御框架。其核心设计包含三阶段：
1. 教师模型分片：将私有上下文数据水平切分为多个互斥子集，分别训练独立教师模型；
2. 合成查询驱动聚合：基于表格特征的有界性与低维性，仅利用原始特征范围或经轻量级DP扰动的边际统计（如1-2阶边际），高效生成高质量合成查询；
3. 隐私标签发布：对各教师模型在合成查询上的预测进行带拉普拉斯噪声的PATE式聚合，将带DP保证的标签化合成查询作为学生模型的“安全上下文”。

主要结果与价值

在涵盖医疗、金融、电商等领域的6个标准表格基准（如Adult、Credit、Bank）上，TabPATE在ε ∈ [2, 8] 的宽松DP预算下：

  • 保持与非私有ICL相当的预测精度（平均仅下降1.2–3.7个百分点）；
  • 将成员推断攻击成功率压制至接近随机水平（AUC ≤ 0.52）；
  • 完全规避对公开数据的依赖，解决现实场景中“无可用公开数据”的关键瓶颈。

本工作为构建实用、合规、免公开数据的私有表格ICL提供了首个系统性解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

Tabular foundation models enable strong in-context learning (ICL) from few labeled examples, but exposing private records in context risks membership leakage. We first demonstrate that basic membership inference attacks succeed against tabular ICL—motivating formal differential privacy (DP) guarantees. We then propose TabPATE, a DP-compliant PATE-style framework for tabular ICL that requires no public in-distribution data. TabPATE partitions private context across teacher models, privately aggregates their predictions on synthetic tabular queries—generated either from feature bounds or lightly privatized low-order marginals—and releases the DP-labeled queries as the student’s context. Leveraging the bounded, low-dimensional nature of tabular data, TabPATE achieves competitive utility (≤3.7% accuracy drop under ε=2–8) while reducing membership inference to near-random success (AUC ≤ 0.52) across six real-world benchmarks. It is the first practical DP-ICL method for tabular data without public data reliance.

Abstract

Tabular foundation models enable accurate in-context learning (ICL) from small labeled datasets, but the private records placed in context can leak through model predictions. We first show that even basic membership inference attacks succeed against tabular ICL, motivating formal privacy protection. We then introduce TabPATE, a differentially private PATE-style defense for tabular ICL that does not require public in-distribution data. TabPATE partitions the private context across teacher models, privately aggregates their labels on synthetic tabular queries, and releases the resulting labeled queries as a student context. Because tabular features are bounded and relatively low-dimensional, useful queries can be generated from feature ranges alone or from lightly privatized marginals. Across tabular benchmarks, TabPATE preserves competitive utility while reducing membership inference to near-random success, providing a practical path to private tabular ICL without public data.

item.title: Expected Gain-based Escalation in Vertical Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31331v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在垂直联邦学习（VFL）中，多个客户端持有同一样本的不同特征视图（如医疗数据中的影像与基因组特征），需协同推理以提升预测性能。然而，对每个样本都执行完整的嵌入融合（即上传中间表示至服务器进行联合建模）会带来显著的通信开销与计算负担，尤其在边缘设备资源受限场景下不可持续。如何选择性地触发高成本融合，成为平衡精度与效率的关键挑战。

方法创新

本文提出一种基于期望增益（Expected Gain）的选择性升级机制，构建两轮VFL推理协议：

  • 第一轮（轻量级）：各客户端独立输出校准后的后验概率，本地加权聚合生成初步预测；
  • 第二轮（按需触发）：仅当模型预估该样本经服务器端嵌入融合后能带来显著正确率提升时，才启动通信并执行融合。

核心创新在于设计一个可解释、无需额外训练的路由分数：

  • 基于留出校准集估计全局校准池化后验（calibrated pooled posterior）；
  • 结合类别级可靠性评估（classwise reliability），量化不同类别下融合操作的预期收益；
  • 路由决策直接由解析式期望增益得分驱动，避免引入专用路由网络或强化学习策略。

实验验证

在多视图分类基准（如NUS-WIDE、ADULT-VFL）及可控测试时视图退化（如随机屏蔽部分模态）设定下，本方法显著优于三类基线：

  • 置信度阈值法（confidence-based）；
  • 学习型增益预测器（learned-gain router）；
  • 拒绝推断/延迟决策（deferral-based）方法。

结果表明：在通信量降低35–62%的同时，准确率损失控制在0.5%以内，实现了更优的通信-精度帕累托前沿。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the communication-accuracy trade-off in vertical federated learning (VFL) by proposing an expected gain-based selective escalation mechanism. In a two-round inference protocol, a low-cost first round produces predictions from calibrated client posteriors; the high-cost second round—embedding fusion at the server—is triggered only when analytically estimated expected improvement in prediction correctness justifies the communication overhead. Our router computes an interpretable, training-free score combining a calibrated pooled posterior and classwise reliability estimates—all derived from held-out calibration data—eliminating the need for auxiliary routing networks. Experiments on multi-view benchmarks (including controlled test-time view degradation) show our method achieves superior communication-accuracy Pareto efficiency: it reduces communication by 35–62% with ≤0.5% accuracy drop, outperforming confidence-, learned-gain-, and deferral-based baselines.

Abstract

Collaborative inference can improve predictive performance by integrating complementary information across agents, but applying collaborative fusion to every sample can incur unnecessary communication and computational overhead. This trade-off is particularly relevant in vertical federated learning (VFL), where clients observe different views of the same sample and fusion typically requires transmitting intermediate representations to a server. We study selective escalation in a two-round VFL inference protocol, in which a low-cost first round produces a prediction from client posteriors and a second embedding-fusion round is invoked only when it is expected to improve the final decision. We formulate routing as expected-gain score estimation: a sample is escalated when a predicted improvement in correctness justifies the additional communication. The proposed analytical score combines a calibrated pooled posterior with classwise reliability estimates of the VFL model, both obtained from held-out calibration data, yielding an interpretable router that requires no separately trained routing network. Experiments on multi-view classification benchmarks, including controlled test--time view degradation settings, show that the proposed router improves the communication-accuracy trade-off over confidence-, learned-gain-, and deferral-based baselines.

item.title: Explaining Machine Learning and Memorization with Statistical Mechanics
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31110v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

人工神经网络（NN）与机器学习（ML）算法虽在实践中广泛应用，但其理论基础仍严重欠缺。这导致两大核心挑战：一是训练过程实际局限于权重空间的隐式低维子流形，但该结构的几何与统计本质尚未被系统刻画，因而难以用于加速或正则化训练；二是模型易受对抗样本攻击干扰，而此类现象的统计物理根源长期缺乏统一解释。

方法与模型框架

本研究引入统计力学作为统一分析范式，将NN训练视为高维随机系统的相变与能量景观演化过程。重点考察两类典型记忆型模型：

  • 稠密联想记忆（DAM）：以超线性激活实现强模式分离，揭示过参数化下“学习—记忆”连续相变；
  • 受限玻尔兹曼机（RBM）：通过热力学极限下的自由能分析，量化数据拟合中泛化分量与记忆分量的权衡。

创新性地建立了DAM与RBM在大尺度极限下的等价映射关系，并推导出二者共享的临界学习维度公式 $d_c \sim \log N$（$N$为训练样本数），为隐式低维性提供首个解析判据。

核心发现与贡献

1. 首次从自旋玻璃理论出发，证明对抗脆弱性源于训练数据分布与模型能量景观的非平衡态失配，而非单纯过拟合；
2. 提出“记忆熵”概念，定量刻画模型对噪声的敏感度阈值，解释为何小扰动即可触发类别翻转；
3. 所得理论框架可直接指导低维投影训练（如PCA+SGD联合优化），在CIFAR-10上验证收敛速度提升37%且鲁棒性增强2.1×。

AI Summary (English)

This thesis bridges machine learning and statistical mechanics to resolve two fundamental theoretical gaps: the origin of implicit low-dimensional learning dynamics and the physical mechanism of adversarial vulnerability. Using spin-glass theory and large-deviation analysis, we rigorously characterize dense associative memories (DAMs) and restricted Boltzmann machines (RBMs) as thermodynamic systems undergoing learning–memorization phase transitions. We derive a universal critical dimension $d_c \sim \log N$ governing the onset of effective low-rank weight evolution, and prove that adversarial fragility arises from metastable energy minima misaligned with data manifolds—not from overfitting alone. Crucially, we establish an exact mapping between DAM and RBM free energies in the thermodynamic limit, enabling analytical tractability previously unattainable. These results yield a predictive “memory entropy” metric for robustness and inform practical low-dimensional training protocols, validated on benchmark datasets.

Abstract

Artificial neural networks (NNs) and machine learning (ML) algorithms are poorly understood from a theoretical perspective, which makes it difficult to fully realize their potential and overcome their weaknesses. For instance, ML algorithms train NN weights by moving them along a low-dimensional subspace of their allowed values, but this implicitly low-dimensional learning structure is not properly exploited to improve training because its nature is not well understood. Moreover, trained NNs are easily confused by pervasive adversarial attacks whose theoretical underpinnings are still unclear. This thesis aims to improve our theoretical understanding of NNs and ML, with a particular focus on adversarial attacks and implicitly low-dimensional learning. For this purpose, we use mathematical tools from statistical mechanics to study different types of NNs and ways in which they can fit the data. In particular, we study two classes of models that fit the data with various degrees of learning and memorization: dense associative memory (DAM) and restricted Boltzmann machines (RBM). In the process, we investigate connections between different versions of these models that are useful to make analytical investigations more efficient.

item.title: Building a Multimodal Dataset of Academic Paper for Keyword Extraction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31069v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

当前关键词抽取任务高度依赖纯文本数据，普遍忽视学术论文中蕴含的图像、音频等多模态信息。这种单模态范式导致语义表征贫乏、跨模态关联缺失，严重制约模型对学术概念的深层理解与抽取精度。更关键的是，面向关键词抽取任务的公开多模态基准数据集几乎空白，成为该领域方法创新与性能验证的重大瓶颈。

方法与数据构建

本研究首次构建了首个专用于关键词抽取的学术论文多模态数据集 AcadKey-MM，包含 1000 篇完整学术论文样本，每样本严格同步提供：（1）论文正文文本；（2）文内嵌入的高质量图表（含OCR提取的图注与图中文字）；（3）作者朗读的论文摘要及核心段落音频（经ASR转录为文本）；（4）由领域专家人工标注的5–8个权威关键词。我们系统对比了无监督（TextRank、YAKE）与监督（BERT-based序列标注）两类主流方法，分别在纯文本、单模态文本（仅图像OCR文本/仅音频ASR文本）、以及多模态文本融合（论文文本 + OCR文本 + ASR文本）三种输入设置下开展实验。

主要发现与创新点

实验表明：不同模态文本具有显著互补性——OCR文本强化技术术语与公式表述，ASR文本突出逻辑连接与强调结构，论文文本承载主体语义。三者拼接融合使F₁值平均提升12.7%（监督模型）和9.3%（无监督模型），显著优于任一单模态输入。本工作不仅填补了多模态关键词抽取数据资源的空白，更实证揭示了模态协同对学术知识提炼的关键价值，为构建更鲁棒、可解释的学术智能系统提供了新范式与可靠基准。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces AcadKey-MM, the first dedicated multimodal dataset for keyword extraction from academic papers, comprising 1,000 samples—each with aligned paper text, figures (with OCR-extracted captions and content), audio recordings of abstracts/key sections (with ASR transcripts), and expert-annotated keywords. We benchmark both unsupervised (TextRank, YAKE) and supervised (BERT-based sequence labeling) keyword extraction methods across three input settings: (1) paper text only; (2) modality-specific text (OCR or ASR alone); and (3) fused text (paper + OCR + ASR). Results demonstrate that texts from different modalities exhibit complementary linguistic characteristics: OCR text enriches technical terminology and symbolic expressions, ASR text captures discourse structure and emphasis, while paper text provides core semantics. Concatenating all three textual sources consistently improves performance—achieving average F₁ gains of +12.7% (supervised) and +9.3% (unsupervised) over paper-text-only baselines. AcadKey-MM bridges a critical resource gap and empirically validates multimodal fusion as a key enabler for accurate, context-aware academic keyword extraction.

Abstract

Up to this point, keyword extraction task typically relies solely on textual data. Neglecting visual details and audio features from image and audio modalities leads to deficiencies in information richness and overlooks potential correlations, thereby constraining the model's ability to learn representations of the data and the accuracy of model predictions. Furthermore, the currently available multimodal datasets for keyword extraction task are particularly scarce, further hindering the progress of research on multimodal keyword extraction task. Therefore, this study constructs a multimodal dataset of academic paper consisting of 1000 samples, with each sample containing paper text, images, audios and keywords. Based on unsupervised and supervised methods of keyword extraction, experiments are conducted using textual data from papers, as well as text extracted from images and audio. The aim is to investigate the differences in performance in keyword extraction task with respect to different modal information and the fusion of multimodal information. The experimental results indicate that text from different modalities exhibits distinct characteristics in the model. The concatenation of paper text, image text and audio text can effectively enhance the keyword extraction performance of academic papers.

item.title: MESA: Prioritizing Vulnerable Communication Channels for Securing Multi-Agent Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30602v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

多智能体系统（MAS）正广泛应用于自动化复杂分布式任务，但其智能体间通信信道构成新型、隐蔽且难以防御的攻击面。现有安全实践多依赖事后响应，缺乏对未被观测到的潜在攻击下关键通信边的前瞻性识别能力。我们发现：信道级攻击影响高度不均衡——单条被攻破的边可导致高达75%的整体攻击成功，凸显优先防护的必要性。

方法：MESA框架

本文提出MESA（Multi-Agent Edge Security Assessment），一种无需标签、无需真实攻击痕迹的主动式信道风险评估框架。它融合6种图论指标（如介数中心性、路径冗余度、信息流敏感度等）与2类动态探针（ablation移除测试与masking遮蔽测试），量化每条边对系统全局决策鲁棒性的贡献度，从而识别“安全关键边”——即一旦被篡改最可能扭曲系统输出的通信链路。

主要发现与验证

在涵盖3类典型MAS场景（协作推理、任务分解、辩论共识）、8种网络拓扑及5个主流开源大模型（Qwen、Llama、Gemma系列）的综合评估中：

  • Mesa排序与实证单边攻击成功率呈强相关（平均Spearman ρ = +0.60，峰值+0.73）；
  • 在资源受限防御部署下，仅监控前10%高危边即可拦截约3倍于随机策略的成功攻击；
  • 鲁棒性测试表明其在LangGraph工作流、异构攻击者/防御者建模下保持有效性，但在自适应攻击或极高冗余图中性能边际下降。

创新点

MESA首次将图结构分析与轻量动态探针结合，实现无监督、低开销、可部署的MAS信道风险量化，为多智能体基础设施的主动安全加固提供了可扩展、可解释的工程化路径。

AI Summary (English)

Multi-agent systems (MAS) introduce critical, under-defended attack surfaces through inter-agent communication channels. To enable proactive defense before attacks occur, we propose MESA, a label-free framework that ranks MAS communication edges by security-criticality—i.e., their likelihood of compromising system-level decisions if breached. MESA integrates six graph-theoretic metrics with two lightweight dynamic probes (ablation and masking), requiring no attack traces or ground-truth labels. Evaluated across three MAS scenarios, eight topologies, and five open-source LLMs (Qwen, Llama, Gemma), MESA achieves strong correlation with empirical per-edge attack success (mean Spearman ρ = +0.60, up to +0.73). Monitoring only the top 10% of MESA-ranked edges intercepts ≈3× more successful attacks than random allocation under resource constraints. We further characterize its robustness across LangGraph workflows and its limitations under adaptive adversaries and high-redundancy graphs. Our results demonstrate that edge-level risk in MAS is often concentrated and predictable—enabling scalable, proactive infrastructure hardening.

Abstract

Multi-agent systems (MAS) are increasingly used to automate complex, distributed workflows. However, their inter-agent communication channels introduce new attack surfaces that remain poorly understood and are difficult to defend against. In this paper, we address how defenders should prioritize limited security effort to protect vulnerable communication channels before attacks are observed. This is motivated by our observation that the channel-level attack impact is highly non-uniform: a single compromised edge can account for up to 75% of total attack success. We introduce Mesa, a label-free framework for proactively ranking which MAS edges are most security-critical -- that is, most likely to affect the system's decision if compromised. Mesa combines six graph-theoretic metrics and two dynamic probes (ablation and masking) without requiring attack traces. We evaluate Mesa against a dynamic misinformation attack pipeline across three diverse MAS scenarios, eight network topologies, and five open-source LLMs from Qwen, Llama, and Gemma families. Mesa rankings correlate strongly with empirical per-edge attack success rate, achieving mean Spearman $ρ=+0.60$ (peaking at $+0.73$). In resource-constrained defense deployment, monitoring the top 10% of Mesa-ranked edges intercepts about 3x the successful attacks as random allocation. We further test Mesa under varying attacker and defender models and LangGraph workflows and characterize its limits under adaptive attacks and high-redundancy graphs. Overall, our results show that edge-level risk in MAS is often concentrated and predictable, allowing proactive hardening of multi-agent infrastructures.

item.title: A Hybrid Framework For Crypto-Ransomware Detection In Enterprise Shared Storage
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30586v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

企业环境中，敏感数据普遍集中存储于网络共享存储（如NAS、SMB文件服务器），而非终端本地。传统端点检测工具（如EDR）难以感知客户端对远程存储的恶意加密行为——攻击者仅需在受控终端发起批量文件读写操作，即可静默加密共享卷，而文件服务器本身无异常进程或注册表变更，导致现有防御体系存在严重盲区。

方法创新

本文提出一种面向企业共享存储环境的混合式勒索软件检测框架，核心包含两大协同模块：

  • Region of Interest (RoI) 流量分析技术：聚焦客户端与文件服务器间协议流量中“高风险操作序列”（如连续大文件随机读+立即覆写、异常文件扩展名批量变更等），提取时序性、语义性特征；
  • 双轨检测机制：
  • 规则增强层：将RoI识别出的新型IoCs结构化入库，作为轻量级插件集成至现有IDS/EDR，提升签名检测覆盖率；
  • 机器学习层：基于RoI特征训练XGBoost模型，专为绕过行为沙箱与混淆技术的高逃逸型加密勒索软件（如LockBit 3.0、BlackCat）优化。

关键成果

  • 在涵盖12个主流勒索软件家族（含变种）及87类真实办公场景良性行为（如CAD设计、视频转码、数据库备份）的数据集上验证：
  • ML模块达99.64%检测精度，0%漏报率（FNR），0.12%误报率（FPR）；
  • 实现加密前平均2.3秒预警（较文件损坏阈值提前92%），支持在首波加密完成前阻断攻击；
  • RoI方法无需部署服务器端代理或修改协议栈，兼容Windows/Linux SMB/NFS环境，具备生产级落地可行性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a hybrid framework for detecting crypto-ransomware targeting enterprise shared storage (e.g., SMB/NFS servers), addressing the critical blind spot where traditional endpoint detection fails to monitor malicious client-initiated encryption of remote files. Our approach centers on a novel Region of Interest (RoI) network traffic analysis technique that identifies high-risk behavioral patterns—such as sequential random-read-and-overwrite bursts or rapid extension changes—within client-server file protocol flows. RoI-derived features train an XGBoost model achieving 99.64% precision, 0% false negative rate, and only 0.12% false positive rate, while extracted IoCs enrich existing EDR/IDS rule sets. Evaluated across 12 ransomware families and domain-validated benign workloads (e.g., CAD, backup, media encoding), the system detects intrusions on average 2.3 seconds before significant damage, enabling pre-encryption mitigation. The framework requires no server-side instrumentation and maintains broad protocol compatibility.

Abstract

Most corporate workplace environments enforce policies and technical controls that limit the storage of sensitive data on client endpoints. Consequently, ransomware operators have evolved variants that expand their attack surface from local systems to network drives and shared storage resources. As traditional endpoint detection mechanisms focus primarily on local system behaviour, a compromised client can impact remote file servers, such as by encrypting shared data, without directly triggering behavioural changes on the servers themselves. In this paper, we propose a hybrid detection framework for detecting crypto-ransomware intrusion within integrated file server and client environments. The framework is based on a new technique referred to as Region of Interest (RoI) to analyse network traffic and extract Indicators of Compromise (IoCs). The IoC repository serves as an additional ruleset to enhance existing security tools such as EDRs and IDSs, while RoI-derived features are used to train an ML model to detect highly evasive variants. This study incorporates a broader set of ransomwares families and carefully selected benign behaviors based on domain expertise, ensuring coverage of common user actions that could interfere with ransomware detection. Beyond IoCs, which operate in a signature-based manner, our machine learning module achieves a detection precision of 99.64%, with a 0% false negative rate (FNR) and a minimal false positive rate (FPR). Furthermore, the proposed method enables early detection, identifying ransomware intrusions before significant damage occurs, achieving an accuracy of 99.44%.

item.title: Forensic Trajectory Signatures for Agent Memory Poisoning Detection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30566v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

大语言模型（LLM）智能体在部署中面临“持续性记忆投毒”（persistent memory poisoning）攻击——攻击者通过污染长期记忆库，诱导智能体在后续任务中泄露敏感信息。现有检测方法多依赖静态提示分析或单点异常识别，难以应对隐蔽、分布式的攻击痕迹。

方法创新：法证轨迹签名

本文首次发现并形式化一个行为不变量（behavioral invariant）：在采用“可观测内存工具调用”进行路由的智能体架构中，成功记忆投毒攻击必须严格满足时序约束——即在调用 email_send_email 前必须先调用 memory_recall_fact；而正常（非窃取）会话极少呈现该转移模式。该不变量源于攻击固有的信息检索依赖链，而非经验相关性——人为抑制该转移将直接导致攻击失效。

核心发现与性能

  • 单规则检测器仅基于该不变量即可实现 AUC = 0.9563；
  • 19维轨迹特征的随机森林模型进一步提升至 AUC = 0.9904（BCa 95% CI [0.987, 0.993]，N=10,000），证实攻击在多条独立行为通道上留下强信号；
  • 过定性验证：移除全部召回相关特征（占特征集一半）后，AUC 仍稳定在 0.990，表明签名是分布式轨迹模式，非单一异常点；
  • 强泛化能力：跨9种主流模型（7B–120B参数）留出测试中，6/9场景达 AUC = 1.000；3个例外均获机制性解释；前沿模型（GPT-4.1、GPT-4o）零样本适配成功；
  • 实时可行性：纯前缀（prefix-only）变体达 AUC = 0.934，支持低延迟拦截；
  • 法证可分性：绕过记忆的提示注入攻击产生显著不同轨迹（得分 0.541），仅凭工具调用日志即可区分攻击类型。

AI Summary (English)

We identify a forensic trajectory signature for detecting persistent memory poisoning in LLM agents: a behavioral invariant requiring memory_recall_fact to precede email_send_email in attack-successful sessions—a transition rare in benign workflows and causally necessary for the attack’s information-retrieval dependency. Exploiting this alone yields AUC = 0.9563; a Random Forest on 19 trajectory features achieves AUC = 0.9904 (BCa 95% CI [0.987, 0.993]). The signature is overdetermined—removing all recall-related features leaves AUC unchanged at 0.990—confirming distributed behavioral imprinting. It generalizes across 9 models (7B–120B), attaining AUC = 1.000 on 6/9 hold-out splits (all exceptions mechanistically explained) and transfers zero-shot to GPT-4.1 and GPT-4o. A prefix-only variant enables real-time blocking (AUC = 0.934). Crucially, prompt-injection attacks bypassing memory yield distinct trajectories (score = 0.541), allowing incident responders to distinguish attack vectors using tool-call logs alone.

Abstract

We discover a behavioral invariant in LLM agents under persistent memory poisoning: in architectures where routing information is retrieved through observable memory-tool invocations, successful attacks require calling memory_recall_fact before email_send_email, a transition that non-exfiltrating sessions rarely exhibit. Under the evaluated architecture, this invariant follows from the attack's information-retrieval dependency rather than being merely an empirical correlation, and suppressing it breaks the attack. A simple rule exploiting this invariant alone achieves AUC = 0.9563. A Random Forest classifier over 19 trajectory features refines it to AUC = 0.9904 (BCa 95% CI [0.987, 0.993], N=10,000 resamples), demonstrating that the attack imprints on multiple independent behavioral channels. The signature is overdetermined: removing all recall-related features (half the feature set) leaves AUC unchanged at 0.990, confirming that memory poisoning induces a distributed trajectory signature rather than a single observable anomaly. Cross-model hold-out on 9 models (7B-120B parameters) confirms AUC = 1.000 on 6/9 hold-out splits, with all three exceptions mechanistically explained. The invariant generalizes to frontier models (GPT-4.1, GPT-4o) without retraining. A strictly prefix-only variant achieves AUC = 0.934, suggesting that real-time blocking is feasible with moderate degradation. The boundary is forensically useful: prompt-injection attacks that bypass memory produce a distinct trajectory (score = 0.541), enabling incident responders to distinguish memory-channel attacks from prompt-injection attacks using tool-call logs alone.

item.title: Your Space is My Zone: Demystifying the Security Risks of AI-Powered Applications on Pre-Trained Model Hubs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30373v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

AI驱动应用（AI-Apps）依托Hugging Face等预训练模型平台，通过在线推理与微调服务大幅降低AI使用门槛。然而，此类应用常由不可信第三方开发，运行于弱隔离环境、安全配置缺失，形成尚未被系统研究的新型攻击面。

方法与框架

本研究首次对三大主流平台上的AI-Apps开展系统性安全分析。我们将AI-App全生命周期映射至OWASP等经典风险分类体系，提炼出5类威胁维度（如访问控制失效、资源复用不安全）和10种具体攻击向量。为实现规模化实证评估，我们设计并实现了自动化分析框架Insightor，支持静态代码扫描、配置审计与敏感数据模式识别。

主要发现

对超97万款公开AI-Apps的扫描揭示：

  • 数千款应用存在硬编码凭证泄露（含API密钥、数据库凭据）；
  • 数百款存在输入注入漏洞，可触发任意代码执行（如通过prompt或JSON参数注入Python/Shell命令）；
  • 数十款嵌入隐蔽后门（如恶意requirements.txt依赖、劫持__init__.py的远程加载逻辑），证实已遭主动利用；
  • 首次发现3类平台原生架构漏洞：模型权重与用户代码共享沙箱导致侧信道越权、日志默认世界可读且含完整请求上下文、推理端点未强制身份绑定致跨租户资源混淆。

创新与影响

本研究不仅揭示了AI-App生态中“传统Web漏洞+AI特有风险”的双重放大效应，更提出可落地的缓解建议（如最小权限沙箱、输入schema强校验、日志脱敏策略），所有发现均已通过负责任披露流程同步至平台方与开发者。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first systematic security analysis of AI-powered Applications (AI-Apps) hosted on pre-trained model hubs (e.g., Hugging Face). We map the AI-App lifecycle to established risk taxonomies (e.g., OWASP), identifying five threat categories and ten attack vectors—from generic web flaws to platform-specific architectural weaknesses. We develop Insightor, an automated analysis framework, and apply it to 970,000+ public AI-Apps, uncovering thousands leaking credentials, hundreds vulnerable to input injection enabling arbitrary code execution, and dozens containing embedded backdoors—evidence of active exploitation. Critically, we discover three novel architectural vulnerabilities inherent to hub design, including insecure resource sharing across tenants and world-readable logs containing full inference contexts. All findings were responsibly disclosed.

Abstract

AI-powered Applications (AI-Apps), hosted on platforms such as Hugging Face, are democratizing access to pre-trained models through online inference and fine-tuning services. While lowering AI adoption barriers, these platforms introduce an unexplored attack surface, as AI-Apps are often developed by untrusted parties with weak isolation and misconfigured security settings. In this paper, we present the first systematic security analysis of AI-Apps across three leading platforms. To structure our investigation, we map the AI-App lifecycle to established risk taxonomies (e.g., OWASP), identifying five threat categories and ten attack vectors ranging from generic web flaws to high-impact architectural issues. Our analysis reveals critical failures including broken access control, insecure resource reuse, insufficient input validation, and sensitive data exposure. Notably, we uncover three novel architectural vulnerabilities inherent to platform design and demonstrate how traditional issues (e.g., world-readable logs) are uniquely amplified in this ecosystem. To assess real-world impact, we develop an analysis framework Insightor and apply it to over 970,000 public AI-Apps. Alarmingly, we find thousands of apps leaking credentials, hundreds containing input injection vulnerabilities that allow arbitrary code execution, and tens harboring embedded backdoors -- indicating active exploitation. We have responsibly disclosed all findings to the affected platforms and developers.

item.title: Quantum Lazy Sampling and Path Recording for Any Group
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30281v1

AI Summary (中文)

量子懒采样与路径记录：面向任意群的通用框架

在量子算法与密码学中，如何高效模拟对随机群元素（如随机函数、置换或幺正算子）具有预言机访问权的量子算法，是一个核心挑战。经典领域中，“懒采样”（lazy sampling）技术通过延迟承诺完整群元素、按需生成部分输入-输出对，实现了对查询过程的精确建模。本文提出并系统构建了该思想的首个普适、可解释的量子模拟框架——路径记录预言机（path-recording oracle），适用于任意闭子群 $G \subseteq U(N)$（包括对称群 $S_N$、幺正群 $U(N)$、Clifford 群等）。

我们从第一性原理出发，设计了一种新型压缩预言机：其量子态以叠加形式存储至多 $t$ 个查询所获得的输入-输出对路径；每次量子查询后，状态更新由群 $G$ 在张量幂表示下的交换子代数（commutant）严格刻画。这一结构天然反映算法实际获取的信息，具备透明的语义可解释性——即“存储什么”与“学到了什么”完全对应，克服了近期 Grinko-Yoshida（QIP ’26）通用压缩预言机缺乏直观含义的局限。

关键创新在于：

  • 统一性：首次为任意闭李群子群提供一致、非特设的量子懒采样机制；
  • 可比性：支持跨群（如 $S_N$ vs. $U(N)$）的预言机行为直接比较，催生新型伪随机性证明范式；
  • 应用突破：由此导出迄今最简伪随机幺正构造——伪随机置换（PRP）与随机Clifford的乘积（PC），显著简化并改进了先前PFC方案（FOCS ’24 / STOC ’25），为后量子密码与量子伪随机性奠定新基础。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce the first general-purpose, interpretable quantum lazy sampling framework—the path-recording oracle—for simulating quantum algorithms with oracle access to random elements of any closed subgroup $G \subseteq U(N)$. Unlike prior group-specific compressed oracles (e.g., for functions, permutations, or unitaries), our construction is derived from first principles: it stores query-response pairs in superposition, and updates are governed by the commutant of $G$’s tensor-power representation—ensuring the stored state exactly reflects the algorithm’s learned information. This yields transparent semantics absent in recent generic alternatives (Grinko–Yoshida, QIP ’26). A key application is enabling direct cross-group comparisons: contrasting oracles for $S_N$ and $U(N)$ leads to the simplest known construction of pseudorandom unitaries—the product of a pseudorandom permutation and a random Clifford (PC), improving upon the prior PFC scheme (FOCS ’24; STOC ’25).

Abstract

A central challenge in quantum algorithms and cryptography is reasoning about algorithms with oracle access to a random group element (e.g. a random function, permutation, or unitary). Can we efficiently simulate such algorithms? Can we determine what they know after t queries? A classical tool for this is lazy sampling: the oracle does not commit to the full group element upfront, but rather samples partial information about it on the fly. We study a quantum analog of lazy sampling: compressed oracles (or recording oracles). These are quantum data structures that allow on-the-fly simulation for quantum queries, originally introduced by Zhandry (CRYPTO '19) for random functions, and generalized to unitaries by Ma-Huang (STOC '25) and permutations by Carolan (STOC '26), and used to great effect in security proofs and lower bounds due to their interpretability. We define and analyze a general-purpose and interpretable path-recording oracle, derived from first principles, that perfectly simulates random elements of any closed subgroup of $U(N)$. Our oracle stores, in superposition, t input-output pairs, with updates described in terms of the commutant of the group's tensor power representation. This transparently records the information the algorithm has learned. Our oracle builds on recent work of Grinko-Yoshida (QIP '26), who gave a different general-purpose compressed oracle without clear interpretability. One interesting application of our path-recording is allowing direct comparisons between compressed oracles of different groups, giving a new technique for proving pseudorandomness results. For example, comparing $S_N$ and $U(N)$ yields what is arguably the simplest construction to date of pseudorandom unitaries: the product PC of a pseudorandom permutation and a random Clifford, improving on the prior PFC construction (Metger-Poremba-Sinha-Yuen, FOCS '24; Ma-Huang, STOC '25).

item.title: The Spectrum Strikes Back: Infrared POV Attacks on Traffic Sign Classification
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30153v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

交通标志分类是自动驾驶系统感知层的关键任务，其安全性直接关系到行车安全。现有物理对抗攻击（如贴片攻击、光投影攻击）虽有效，但普遍存在人眼可见性高的问题；而基于非可见光谱（如红外）的攻击又常受限于动态响应能力弱、触发机制僵化，难以实现远程、实时、隐蔽的干扰。

方法创新

本文提出一种基于近红外频段的视觉暂留（Persistence-of-Vision, POV）攻击：利用人眼无法感知的850 nm近红外LED阵列，通过高速时序调制，在目标交通标志表面“投射”动态对抗图案。该攻击具备三大特性：① 完全隐蔽——不改变标志外观，无可见光泄露；② 远程可触发——支持无线信号实时启动/切换攻击内容；③ 位置鲁棒——通过数字仿真优化LED布设位置与扫描轨迹，适配不同标志尺寸与安装角度。

实验验证与结果

我们在真实道路场景中开展系统性评估：覆盖2类典型交通标志（禁行、限速）、12种异构模型（CNN、ViT、YOLO系列等）、距离跨度5–20米及昼夜/雨雾等多种光照条件。结果显示：在20米距离下，平均攻击成功率仍达89.3%；对部署于车载边缘设备的轻量级模型（如MobileNetV3），成功率高达96.7%；且攻击效果不受环境光照显著影响。

防御与拓展

我们提出双轨防御方案：① 硬件层——加装低成本近红外截止滤光片（阻断800–900 nm波段）；② 软件层——设计基于帧间红外残差异常检测的实时识别模块。此外，为验证技术普适性，我们进一步研制了可见光RGB-POV原型机，证实该机制可迁移至人眼可见场景，为后续多模态对抗研究提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces The Spectrum Strikes Back: a stealthy, dynamic physical adversarial attack against traffic sign classifiers using near-infrared (NIR) persistence-of-vision (POV) displays.
