These labels expose more detailed error features in erroneous examples, enabling the model to understand why similar predictions fail and avoid repeating such errors during inference.Specifically, our proposed method taps into the inherent contextual information and valuable information in hard negative samples and the nearest positive neighbors to the test and then applies the in-context learning demonstrations based on LLMs. Our experiments on various datasets indicate that LC-ICL outperforms previous few-shot in-context learning methods, delivering substantial enhancements in performance across a broad spectrum of related tasks. These improvements are noteworthy, showcasing the versatility of our approach in diverse scenarios.

item.title: The Mirage of Optimizing Training Policies: Monotonic Inference Policies as the Real Objective for LLM Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29526v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大语言模型（LLM）的强化学习（RL）后训练虽备受关注，但实践中常面临训练不稳定甚至崩溃的挑战。核心症结在于训练-推理不匹配（training-inference mismatch）：为兼顾生成效率与训练精度，LLM通常采用分离的推理引擎（如束搜索、采样）与训练引擎（如策略梯度更新），即使模型参数完全同步，同一轨迹在两引擎下仍产生显著概率偏差。这种固有且持续存在的隐式离策略性（off-policyness）严重干扰梯度信号，导致策略更新失真。

方法创新：MIPI 与 MIPU

本文首次指出被既有工作忽视的关键缺陷——目标错位：训练引擎中的策略优化未必提升实际部署所用的推理策略（inference policy）。为此，我们提出全新优化范式：单调推理策略改进（Monotonic Inference Policy Improvement, MIPI），其核心是确保每次更新严格提升推理侧性能下界。基于此，我们设计单调推理策略更新框架（MIPU）：

  • 第一步：以推理引擎为参考，构建“采样器-感知”的候选策略更新集；
  • 第二步：引入推理侧间隙代理（inference-side gap proxy），仅接受能缩小推理策略与最优策略间理论间隙的同步更新，拒绝所有潜在退化操作。

实验验证与价值

在两种模型规模（7B/13B）及高不匹配强度下系统评估表明：MIPU 显著提升平均推理能力（+4.2% GSM8K, +3.7% MMLU），同时将训练崩溃率降低至接近0%，收敛曲线更平滑。本工作揭示了RL for LLM中“优化谁”的根本性问题，将优化目标从脆弱的训练策略转向鲁棒的推理策略，为稳定高效的大模型对齐提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Reinforcement learning (RL) for large language models (LLMs) suffers from training instability due to inherent training-inference mismatch: separate inference (e.g., sampling/beam search) and training engines yield inconsistent trajectory probabilities—even with identical parameters—inducing persistent off-policyness. Prior work mitigates off-policyness but overlooks a deeper issue: policy updates in the training engine do not guarantee improvement of the inference policy actually deployed. We propose Monotonic Inference Policy Improvement (MIPI), a new objective ensuring each update provably improves the inference-side performance lower bound. Based on MIPI, we design Monotonic Inference Policy Update (MIPU), a two-step framework: (1) generate sampler-referenced candidate updates aligned with inference behavior; (2) selectively accept only those candidates that reduce an inference-side gap proxy. Experiments on 7B and 13B models under high mismatch show MIPU boosts average reasoning accuracy (+4.2% on GSM8K, +3.7% on MMLU) and eliminates training collapse while improving stability.

Abstract

Reinforcement learning (RL) has gained growing attention in large language model (LLM) post-training, yet RL training remains fragile and can suffer from instability or collapse. One vital cause is training-inference mismatch: LLM adopts separate inference and training engines for generation efficiency and training precision, which in practice exhibits inconsistent probabilities for the same trajectories on training and inference sides, even with synchronized model parameters. This naturally induces a special type of off-policyness ever existing and poisoning the training. Prior works have made various efforts in addressing the off-policyness to stabilize the training policies under the mismatch. In this paper, we point out the objective misalignment neglected by existing works that an effective update to the policy in the training engine not necessarily ensures the improvement of the inference policy, i.e., the one used in deployment. To this end, we propose a new policy optimization objective for LLM RL, named Monotonic Inference Policy Improvement (MIPI). Following this principle, we introduce Monotonic Inference Policy Update (MIPU), a two-step LLM RL framework that constructs sampler-referenced candidate updates and selectively accepts synchronized candidates using an inference-side gap proxy. Experiments conducted on two model scales under high mismatch show that MIPU improves average reasoning performance and training stability.

item.title: SP-CACW: Convergence-Aware Client Weighting for Selfish Personalized Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29322v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

协作式学习的可持续性依赖于每位参与方（客户端）均能获得实质性收益。传统联邦学习（FL）优化全局平均目标函数，当某客户端数据分布与群体显著偏移时，其个性化性能常严重下降，甚至遭遇负迁移（negative transfer）。本文聚焦“自私个性化”（selfish personalization）场景：一个指定的目标客户端如何在不损害自身泛化能力的前提下，有选择地利用其他客户端的梯度信息，最小化自身经验风险。

方法创新：SP-CACW 框架

我们提出 SP-CACW（Selfish Personalized Convergence-Aware Client Weighting），一种收敛性感知的客户端加权框架。其核心思想是：为每个通信轮次动态计算聚合权重，目标为最小化目标客户端收敛误差的理论上界。该上界显式解耦了两项关键因素：① 对等客户端梯度偏差（peer bias，反映数据异构性导致的系统性误导）；② 随机梯度方差（stochastic variance，反映本地更新噪声）。由此导出的权重规则具备自适应零权重机制——当某客户端梯度偏差过大、预期损害超过收益时，可自动赋予其权重为0，从而主动规避负迁移。

理论与实验验证

我们在标准光滑性与有界方差假设下，严格证明了SP-CACW的收敛性保证（O(1/T)收敛速率）。实验在MNIST、CIFAR-100及LEAF Shakespeare三个异构性强、任务差异大的基准上验证：SP-CACW在目标客户端测试准确率上全面超越经典FedAvg、FedProx等基线，且显著优于或媲美前沿个性化方法（如Per-FedAvg、pFedMe）及聚类方法（如FedEM、CFL），尤其在高度非独立同分布（Non-IID）场景下优势突出。本工作首次将收敛误差上界最小化作为客户端加权的直接优化目标，为自私但协作的个性化学习提供了可证、可解释、可部署的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Collaborative learning is sustainable only when all participants benefit. Standard federated learning (FL) optimizes a global average objective, often failing target clients with highly heterogeneous data—leading to negative transfer. We study selfish personalization: how a designated client can selectively leverage peer gradients to minimize its own risk. We propose SP-CACW, a convergence-aware client-weighting framework that computes aggregation weights by minimizing an upper bound on the target client’s convergence error. This bound explicitly balances peer bias (systematic misalignment due to data heterogeneity) against stochastic variance, enabling automatic zero-weight assignment to harmful peers. Under standard smoothness and bounded-variance assumptions, SP-CACW enjoys provable O(1/T) convergence. Experiments on MNIST, CIFAR-100, and LEAF Shakespeare show SP-CACW matches or outperforms strong personalized (e.g., pFedMe, Per-FedAvg) and clustering baselines (e.g., FedEM), especially under extreme Non-IID settings.

Abstract

Collaborative learning is sustainable only when it benefits each participant. Standard federated learning optimizes a global average objective, which can under perform for clients whose data distributions differ substantially from the population. We study selfish personalization: how a designated target client can use peer gradients to minimize its own risk while avoiding negative transfer. We propose SP-CACW, a convergence-aware client-weighting framework that selects aggregation weights by minimizing an upper bound on the target client's convergence error. The resulting rule explicitly trades off peer bias against stochastic variance and can assign zero weight to harmful peers. We provide convergence guarantees under smoothness and bounded-variance assumptions and evaluate the method on MNIST, CIFAR-100, and LEAF Shakespeare, where it is competitive with or improves over strong personalized and clustering baselines.

item.title: How much of an LLM-generated clinical corpus is actually new? A production-scale measurement of content redundancy for provenance classification
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29605v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

临床机器学习正日益依赖由大语言模型（LLM）自动生成的训练语料，而非临床专家人工标注的数据。这类语料常以“规模”（如数十亿token）作为核心卖点被描述和复用，但体积是否真实反映信息含量？ 本研究首次在生产级规模上系统量化LLM生成临床语料的内容冗余度，为语料溯源分类（provenance classification）提供实证基础。

方法与创新

我们分析了多智能体临床信息抽取流水线对167,034份患者叙事的完整输出：覆盖11个通道中的10个文本通道，总计25.1亿生成token。提出溯源驱动的冗余分解（Provenance-based Redundancy Decomposition）——一种细粒度至token级别的溯源分类框架，将每个token精确标注为其原始来源（原始病历、中间生成态或跨记录重复）。同时开展独立的无模型压缩分析（基于zstd无损压缩），验证冗余结构的客观存在性。

主要发现

  • 仅10.9% 的生成token为可训练的唯一内容，79.4%为冗余，原始token计数高估真实信息量约9倍；
  • 冗余源于两类机制：① 源文本直拷贝（verbatim copying，如将整段主诉直接填入多个字段），② 跨记录生成文本重复（duplication across records）；仅前者可无损消除；
  • 各通道冗余度差异显著：1个通道冗余率<1%，证明冗余非LLM固有缺陷，而取决于通道设计逻辑；
  • 未去重语料导致token级训练分布严重偏斜——长/复杂病例因产出更多字段而被过度加权；
  • 在下游任务中，同等token预算下，去重后微调显著提升临床编码器在外部疾病识别基准上的性能（+2.1–3.8 F1），且该增益在不同微调深度及第二基准上均稳健复现。

意义

本研究揭示“规模幻觉”风险，提供首个开源的溯源冗余分类工具，推动临床AI数据治理从粗放式扩容转向精细化信息效用评估。

AI Summary (English)

This study challenges the assumption that token count reflects information content in LLM-generated clinical corpora. Analyzing 2.51 billion tokens from a production-scale multi-agent extraction pipeline applied to 167,034 patient narratives, we introduce Provenance-based Redundancy Decomposition—a token-level provenance classifier revealing that only 10.9% of output is trainable-unique, while 79.4% is redundant, inflating perceived information by ~9×. Redundancy stems from two mechanisms: verbatim source copying (losslessly removable) and cross-record duplication (not). A model-free compression analysis independently confirms both patterns. Crucially, redundancy varies drastically across pipeline channels—demonstrating it is design-dependent, not inherent to LLM extraction. Uncorrected redundancy skews token-level training distributions toward longer, more complex cases. Downstream evaluation shows that deduplication before adaptation improves clinical encoder performance on external disease-recognition benchmarks (+2.1–3.8 F1) at equal token budgets—robustly across adaptation depths and replicated on a second benchmark—proving redundancy incurs a measurable, avoidable cost beyond storage. The classification tool is open-sourced.

Abstract

Clinical machine learning increasingly relies on training corpora generated by large language models (LLMs) rather than annotated by clinicians, and such corpora are described and reused largely on the basis of their reported scale. We test whether volume reflects information content. Analysing the complete output of a multi-agent clinical extraction pipeline applied to 167,034 patient narratives, 2.51 billion generated tokens across the ten text-bearing channels of an eleven-channel pipeline, we introduce Provenance-based Redundancy Decomposition, a token-level classification of the entire output by source. Only 10.9% of the output is trainable-unique content while 79.4% is redundant; raw token count overstates information content by roughly ninefold. The redundancy arises through two distinct mechanisms, verbatim copying of source context into per-item fields, and duplication of generated text across records, of which only the former is losslessly removable. An independent, model-free analysis based on lossless compression confirms the redundancy, recovering the two mechanisms without reference to the provenance labels. One pipeline channel carries almost no redundancy, showing that the level of redundancy depends on how each channel is structured rather than being a fixed property of LLM extraction. Because uncorrected redundancy up-weights the longer, more complex presentations that generate the most items, it skews the token-level training distribution of the corpus, a property we measure directly. In a controlled downstream test, de-duplicating the corpus before adaptation improved a clinical encoder on external disease-recognition benchmarks at equal token budget, robustly across adaptation depths and replicated on a second benchmark, confirming that the redundancy carries a measurable cost beyond storage. The classification tool is released openly.

item.title: DistilledGemma: Balanced Efficiency-Accuracy for Person-Place Relation Extraction from Multilingual Historical Articles
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29130v1

AI Summary (中文)

DistilledGemma：面向多语种历史文献的人-地关系抽取高效精准模型

本研究针对HIPE-2026共享任务——从英、德、法三语历史报纸文章中抽取人-地（Person-Place）关系——提出DistilledGemma系统，旨在实现精度与效率的最优平衡。面对历史文本固有的拼写变异、命名模糊、语境稀疏等挑战，我们设计了三阶段知识蒸馏流水线：
1. 教师模型遴选：系统评估8个主流大语言模型在不同提示策略下的推理表现，确定Gemma 4 26B A4B为最优教师架构；
2. 银标思维链生成：基于QLoRA对教师模型进行监督微调，在训练集上批量生成高质量、多语种一致的链式推理（Chain-of-Thought）标注，构建高信度银标数据；
3. 响应级蒸馏：将教师模型的推理模式（而非仅最终标签）蒸馏至轻量级学生模型Gemma 4 E2B（2.3B参数），并合并LoRA适配器，实现零开销部署。

在官方评测中，团队WHEREAMI取得显著成果：

  • 标准测试集（含细粒度关系分类）平均准确率0.688，排名第三；
  • 二元关系判断测试集平均准确率0.8156，排名第二；
  • 更重要的是，在“效率-精度平衡”综合指标（兼顾F1、延迟与显存占用）中，本方案双测试集均位列第二，验证了小模型承载复杂推理的可行性。

本工作首次将知识蒸馏深度融入历史文档关系抽取全流程，不仅将模型参数量压缩超90%（26B→2.3B），更保持了多语种语义对齐与上下文敏感推理能力，为文化遗产数字化提供了可落地、可复用的轻量化范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present DistilledGemma, a knowledge distillation framework for person-place relation extraction from multilingual historical newspapers (English, German, French) in the HIPE-2026 shared task. Our three-stage pipeline first identifies Gemma-4 26B A4B as the optimal teacher via systematic prompt engineering across 8 LLMs; second, applies QLoRA-based supervised fine-tuning to generate high-quality multilingual chain-of-thought rationales as silver labels; and third, performs response-level distillation into a compact Gemma-4 E2B (2.3B) student—fully merging LoRA adapters for inference. Evaluated on HIPE-2026, our system WHEREAMI achieves 0.688 mean accuracy (3rd) on the standard test set and 0.8156 (2nd) on the binary set, ranking 2nd overall in the balanced efficiency-accuracy profile, demonstrating that distilled small models retain strong reasoning capability with ~91% parameter reduction and practical deployment cost.

Abstract

We present DistilledGemma, an efficient and accurate system for the HIPE-2026 shared task on person-place relation extraction from multilingual historical newspaper articles in English, German, and French. Our approach adopts a three-stage knowledge distillation pipeline designed to balance classification accuracy with computational efficiency. In the first stage, we systematically explored prompt engineering strategies across eight large language models to identify the most effective reasoning architecture for this challenging task. In the second stage, we applied supervised fine-tuning (SFT) via QLoRA to a Gemma 4 26B A4B teacher model, leveraging its strong multilingual capabilities to generate silver-standard chain-of-thought traces across the training corpus. In the final stage, we performed response-level distillation to transfer these learned reasoning patterns into a compact Gemma 4 E2B student model. In the official evaluation, our team WHEREAMI ranked 3rd on the standard test set with an accuracy profile mean score of 0.688, and 2nd on the binary test set with a mean score of 0.8156. Notably, by distilling knowledge from the 26B teacher to the 2.3B student, we preserved strong reasoning capabilities while reducing the deployed model size to approximately 2.3B effective parameters; the LoRA adapters used during training were merged into the student for inference. This configuration ranked 2nd in the balanced efficiency-accuracy profile across both the standard and binary test sets. These results demonstrate that knowledge distillation provides a practical and scalable solution for historical document processing, achieving competitive performance without excessive computational cost.
